title: A Fresh Start for Your Career: Become a Recruiter
description: A Fresh Start for Your Career: Become a Recruiter \- No Experience Required * **Clear career path – be certain of how to progress** * **Fast\-track to leadership – progress your career in your own terms** * **Promotions attached to performance – the more you succeed, the more you advance** At Access Healthcare, the only thing standing between you and success is your own performance, ambition, and drive. If you bring the talent and determination, the opportunities to grow are limitless. Maybe you’re ready to take the next step in your career, eager to prove yourself in a high\-energy, fast\-paced environment. Or maybe you've been looking for that one role where your people skills, ambition, and determination can truly shine and lead to tangible career growth. This is it. **About Recruitment with Access Healthcare** * **Take Ownership of Your Impact** – Manage your own candidate pipeline, match the right professionals to the right roles, and directly influence patient care across Ireland’s healthcare system. Your efforts drive real\-world results—and your success. * **Work with the Best in Healthcare** – Collaborate with leading hospitals, clinics, and healthcare providers by delivering top\-tier talent into critical roles. You’ll become a trusted expert in an industry where quality truly matters. * **Accelerate Your Growth in a High\-Demand Sector** – Gain hands\-on experience in talent acquisition, candidate management, and strategic delivery. With our structured career path, you'll have every opportunity to level up fast—without the pressure of sales. **What We Offer You** * **Career Progression** – We don’t just fill positions; we build careers. You’ll have clear paths to progress into senior and leadership roles as your expertise grows. * **Professional Development** – Gain access to training, mentorship, and the opportunity to specialize in healthcare recruitment. Your career is always evolving. * **Recognition \& Rewards –** Your achievements won’t go unnoticed. Consistent performance is recognised with greater responsibility, new challenges, and fast\-tracked promotions (4 promotions in 2 years? Here it is possible). * **Leadership Academy \-** Fast\-track your path to management with exclusive access to advanced training, leadership certifications, and mentorship. Our academy is designed to equip top performers with the skills and recognition needed to step confidently into leadership roles. **Uncapped Earnings Potential** : Basic salary \+ commission * Year One **Average** Total Compensation: €45K\+ * Year Two **Average** Total Compensation: €65K\+ **What You Bring** * **2\+ years in a fast\-paced or similar role (any industry can be considered!)** * **Unstoppable ambition and drive for success** * Ability to thrive in a target\-driven, high\-energy environment * A track record of exceeding KPIs and going above and beyond **Still wondering if this role is the right move?** Take Rian’s journey as inspiration – within just 24 months at Access Healthcare, he’s advanced from Recruitment Consultant to Associate Manager. Along the way, he’s honed his expertise and developed a unique leadership style. You could follow a similar path, seizing new opportunities and challenges that keep your career exciting and rewarding. For more information, email your CV at francisca.simoes@accesshealthcare.ie

title: Accountant
description: Our client, a medium sized Dublin based firm are looking for an experienced **Senior Accountant** to join their growing team. The successful Senior Accountant will be ambitious to progress within the practice and provide a high\-quality service to a diverse client base. **Responsibilities** * Effective preparation and completion of client files and financial statements. * Mentoring of junior staff in all aspects of accounts preparation. * Ensuring effective communication with clients and colleagues. * Identify and address client needs with effective solutions. * Liaising with Tax Department in relation to preparation of Income Tax and Corporation Tax returns and ensuring timely filing of same within deadlines. **Requirements** * The Senior Accountant will be **ACCA/ACA/CTA qualified** . * Minimum 1 year post qualification experience in Ireland. * Ability to work on own initiative and provide guidance to others. * Experience in preparing Irish income tax and related returns. * Excellent communication skills and confident conversing with Clients and stakeholders, through all mediums. **Benefits** * Salary **€55,000 \- €65,000** DOE * Hybrid working model * 24 days annual leave * Flexible working hours * Free parking * Social events * Wellbeing initiatives * Annual bonus * Paid CPD obligations If you are an Senior Accountant looking for a new opportunity, please contact Andrew Hendrickx for more information. We do not send out your cv without your permission.

title: Administrator, People Services, EMEA (1 year FTC)
description: **Job Description** This position offers you the opportunity to expand your skills and experience as you support the Digital Realty global HR People Services team through a variety of administrative tasks, data entry updates, answering employee’s questions, managing the HR tickets and more. You'll join the largest data center provider in the world and learn about the department and its services from a highly\-talented and experienced team. As part of the team you'll enjoy the "best of both worlds:" the close\-knit atmosphere of our office as well as the stability and resources of a respected and growing global corporation. We are proactive about helping our people grow and develop, and as Digital Realty continues to grow, we'll be looking for proven players to move up. **What You’ll Do** In this role you will be responsible for maintaining data in our Human Resource Information System (HRIS) ensuring compliance within our systems. Alongside this, you will support employees by monitoring, responding and where required assigning HR tickets to the appropriate contact. **Specifically, You Will Be Responsible For** * Creating and maintaining positions in Oracle Human Capital Management (HCM) * Managing Contractor and Employee records in Oracle HCM, including onboarding, offboarding, promotions, changes and updating other HR data * Managing HR Tickets as tier 1 support and escalating as necessary * Assisting employees to resolve their HR questions * Data validation, including developing, implementing and running audits to ensure data integrity within Oracle * Maintaining and managing right to work validations and coordinating pre\-employment background checks * Assisting with various projects related to onboarding, offboarding and employee data management * Provide exceptional service to our internal customers, including employees, HRBP’s, IT, etc. * Other general administration tasks related to HR Operations and People Services **What You’ll Need** To be a strong fit for the Senior Associate, People Services opportunity, you should have: * \+2 years of related experience. Experience in Human Resources is preferred * Strong organisational and time\-management skills with strict attention to detail * The ability to multitask and manage multiple activities and priorities * A professional and articulate presence and comfort communicating with employees * Proficiency in Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint and Outlook * Strong writing skills including accurate grammar, spelling and punctuation * Excellent customer service skills * The ability to express ideas clearly in written and verbal communications * A strong sense of ownership and goal driven personality * Resourcefulness and the ability to achieve goals independently * The ability to review processes and procedures to identify areas for improvement and automation * Reliability and punctuality * Oracle experience or other HRIS experience a plus * Service Now, JIRA or other ticket management system experience a plus **A Bit About Us** Digital Realty brings companies and data together by delivering the full spectrum of data center, colocation and interconnection solutions. PlatformDIGITAL®, the company's global data center platform, provides customers with a secure data meeting place and a proven Pervasive Datacenter Architecture (PDx™) solution methodology for powering innovation and efficiently managing Data Gravity challenges. Digital Realty gives its customers access to the connected data communities that matter to them with a global data center footprint of 300\+ facilities in 50\+ metros across 25\+ countries on six continents. To learn more about Digital Realty, please visit digitalrealty.com or follow us on LinkedIn and Twitter. **What We Can Offer You** Our rapidly evolving business sector offers the opportunity to be part of a courageous and passionate team who work together to understand and meet the changing needs of our global customers. Join us and you’ll be part of a supportive and inclusive environment where you can bring your whole self to work. As part of our team, you’ll get to work with people from different business areas, challenge the way we do things and put your ideas into action. We’ll also give you plenty of development opportunities so you can build a rewarding and successful career with us. This is an exciting time to join our business so apply now and make your mark on our future. Apply today, take charge of your career and grow your talents with us. Please note this is a 12\-month FTC, only candidates with a maximum 2\-week notice period will be considered for the role.

title: Analyst
description: This is an excellent opportunity for a motivated and inquisitive individual to contribute directly to the CCPC’s mission of ensuring that markets work well for consumers. As an Analyst, you will be exposed to the full spectrum of competition policy, including enforcement, merger review, market analysis, and advocacy. The role provides a unique opportunity to develop a broad understanding of how competition and consumer protection issues affect the Irish economy. You will play an active role on multidisciplinary case teams, working closely with colleagues from legal, economic, investigative, and policy backgrounds. Your work will involve gathering and assessing a wide range of evidence, carrying out research, preparing written analyses, and assisting in the development of recommendations on next steps in cases. You will also have the opportunity to contribute to market studies, regulatory research, stakeholder engagement, and advocacy initiatives designed to improve outcomes for consumers. Depending on the assignment and stage of a case or project, your responsibilities may include: • Conducting structured research into markets, companies, and competitive dynamics. • Analysing documents, data, and other forms of evidence to identify key issues or possible concerns. • Drafting internal memos, reports, and briefing materials for senior colleagues, including the Commission. • Assisting with investigative work such as interviews, requests for information, site visits, or dawn raids. • Supporting the preparation of decision documents, market study reports, and external publications. • Engaging with businesses, consumers, other regulators, and international bodies as part of the CCPC’s advocacy and outreach work. • Contributing to cross\-Divisional teams on complex or high\-profile investigations. The role is varied, intellectually stimulating, and provides genuine opportunities to influence policy and competitive outcomes that matter to the Irish public. Throughout your time with the CCPC, you will benefit from structured training, on\-the\-job learning, and the opportunity to develop skills across analysis, research, regulation, communication, and project management. The CCPC encourage the development of its staff and provides regular opportunities for career progression within the organisation.

title: Analyst (HR)
description: We are recruiting on behalf of a leading firm in Dublin for an **HR Analyst** to join a dynamic HR team. This role is perfect for someone tech\-savvy with strong analytical skills and an interest in developing HR expertise. **Location** : Dublin 4 (4 days on site per week) **Salary** : €40,000\-€45,000 **The Role** Reporting to the HR Business Partner, you will manage HR administration, maintain accurate HR data, support payroll and benefits, and produce reports and dashboards for management. You’ll also contribute to recruitment, regulatory compliance, and process improvement initiatives, combining operational HR with data and technology. **What You’ll Do** * Maintain HR records and ensure data integrity in HR systems. * Support recruitment and payroll processes. * Prepare reports, dashboards, and board pack materials. * Analyse data to provide insights and improve HR processes. * Identify opportunities to automate or streamline HR tasks. **What We’re Looking For** * 1\+ year in a data focused role. * Strong tech skills: Excel (pivot tables), Power BI, HR systems, or analytics tools. * Interest in HR, payroll, or people analytics. * Confident in presentations and management reporting. * Analytical, organised, and proactive with attention to detail. * Previous HR administration, payroll, or generalist experience is a plus. *By applying to this job, your CV will be assessed by a consultant from Recruiters.ie for the position described and your CV may be added to our secure applicant tracking system however your details will never be shared to any other third party without your consent.*

title: Architectural Technician
description: Arcadis is the world's leading company delivering sustainable design, engineering, and consultancy solutions for natural and built assets. We are more than 36,000 people, in over 70 countries, dedicated to improving quality of life. Everyone has an important role to play. With the power of many curious minds, together we can solve the world’s most complex challenges and deliver more impact together. **Role description:** We are seeking a Senior Technical team member to join our Dublin as part of our UK and Ireland Architectural Team, with a focus on a diverse range of projects including Healthcare, Education, Transportation and Mixed\-Use development, both locally and across the UK. This challenge will give you the opportunity to focus on a range of projects for a growing, diverse client base. In this position, you will join a highly creative, collaborative team dedicated to innovative problem solving and design excellence. **Role accountabilities:** * You'll be developing, coordinating and leading the detailed design \& construction phases of projects of varying size, delivering high quality technical solutions, leading elements of complex large and small projects independently and within a team. * Work on all aspects of project delivery \- detailed design development, working drawings, contract administration and on\-site services. * Resolve complex technical and detailed design issues. * Maintain the project documentation, drawings and specifications * Conduct on\-site inspections and document site reviews * Process change control requests during construction and address RFI * Understand and adhere to procedures including quality management and environmental management systems and health \& safety * Promotes the use and continuous development of appropriate systems, procedures and protocols * Supporting the project team with input to programming, resourcing and financial reviews * Work collaboratively with the team and support junior members of staff **Qualifications \& Experience:** * Your sector experience would ideally include recent exposure to health, education and scientific projects in the UK * Ideally MCIAT or regionally equivalent accredited * Experience of working on larger projects as part of a team and day to day leadership for smaller projects * Exemplary Revit ability and experience of complex BIM level 2 projects * Naturally you’ll have a strong knowledge of building regulations, design standards and best practice * Strong organisational skills as well as advanced written / verbal communication skills Why Arcadis? We can only achieve our goals when everyone is empowered to be their best. We believe everyone's contribution matters. It’s why we are pioneering a skills\-based approach, where you can harness your unique experience and expertise to carve your career path and maximize the impact we can make together. You’ll do meaningful work, and no matter what role, you’ll be helping to deliver sustainable solutions for a more prosperous planet. Make your mark, on your career, your colleagues, your clients, your life and the world around you. Together, we can create a lasting legacy. Join Arcadis. Create a Legacy. Our Commitment to Equality, Diversity, Inclusion \& Belonging We want you to be able to bring your best self to work every day which is why we take equality and inclusion seriously and hold ourselves to account for our actions. Our ambition is to be an employer of choice and provide a great place to work for all our people. We believe that by working together diverse people with different experiences develop the most innovative ideas. Equality, diversity and inclusion is at the heart of how we improve quality of life and we work closely with our people across six ED\&I Workstreams: Age, Disability, Faith, Gender, LGBT\+ and Race. A diverse and skilled workforce is essential to our success.

title: Associate Chiropractor
description: EXCITING OPPORTUNITY TO WORK IN IRELAND’S LARGEST CHIROPRACTIC COMPANY \- ASSOCIATE CHIROPRACTOR POSITION IN OUR DUBLIN LOCATIONS ABOUT CHIROPRACTIX CHIROPRACTIX is Ireland’s Largest Chiropractic Company, with multiple locations throughout the country in cities and countryside. We are a growing company with new locations being added regularly. We are searching for motivated practitioners to come and join our already existing team of over 30 staff members across Our locations. As a Company we like to nurture Our Team Members and appreciate practitioners bringing flare and their professional interests to the table. WHAT WE OFFER \- EU and Non\-EU Passport Holders Accepted \- High Remuneration \- Average Practitioner Remuneration €34,000 \- €90,000 with ability to earn more \- High Volume of New Patients across all clinics \- Favourable working hours \- Full time \- 35 Hours per week \- Part time \- Flexible working hours available \- Full Training Provided for Qualified Practitioners so experience not necessary \- In\-House CPD courses \- Team working environment \- Mentorship Program for New Graduates \- Corporate Clients such as VHI, Indeed.com, Paypal, Bank of America, Workday and more HOW TO APPLY If you would like to come and join our Team, please forward a CV and Cover letter to work@chiropractix.ieand to see more about our clinics please visit www.chiropractix.ie or check out our socials on Instagram and Facebook @chiropractixhq Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €34,000\.00\-€90,000\.00 per year Language: * English (required) Licence/Certification: * Chiropractic Certification (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Backend Engineer/API, Payments and Risk
description: Note: if you are an intern, new grad, or staff applicant, please do not apply using this link and visit our jobs page for those specific postings. Who we are **About Stripe** Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies \- from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups \- use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone's reach while doing the most important work of your career. **About The Organization** The Payments organization focuses on developing products and platforms that enable users to accept payments from customers efficiently. This includes building APIs for processing payments, enabling regional, non\-card payment options, and extending Stripe's capabilities to make it easy for businesses to accept in\-person payments. The Risk Engineering team develops products that minimize financial and regulatory risks while ensuring a seamless user experience, thereby safeguarding Stripe’s brand and financial stability. Team Matching: exact team matching for one of the subteams within this org will begin during final stages. Please note we may also consider you for different orgs based on your experience, location, etc. More information on our team matching process can be found here. What you’ll do We’re looking for Backend engineers who want to make an impact on managing money at a global scale with a passion for building ergonomic APIs. You’ll play a key role in extending our balance management platform and in building out a new funds accessibility platform leveraged by enterprises and SMBs alike. Our team collaborates with many cross\-functional teams – from Infrastructure to Product – at Stripe to deliver innovative solutions that address evolving user needs. **Responsibilities** * Scope, design, build, and maintain APIs, services, and large\-scale systems that reliably and efficiently handle billions of money movement requests * Debug and solve critical production issues across services and multiple levels of the stack * Mentor engineers to help them grow * Collaborate with stakeholders across the company to build new features at large\-scale, while improving internal engineering standards, tooling, and processes * Collaborate effectively in a distributed and hybrid team, maintaining open communication and strong connections with colleagues Who you are We're looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. **Minimum Requirements** * 2\-12\+ years of industry software engineering experience (does not include internships nor includes co\-ops) * Strong coding skills in any programming language (we understand new languages can be learned on the job so our interview process is language agnostic) * Strong collaboration skills, can work across workstreams within your team and contribute to your peers’ success * Have the ability to thrive on a high level of autonomy, responsibility, and think of yourself as entrepreneurial * Interest in working as a generalist across varying technologies and stacks to solve problems and delight both internal and external users **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience with large\-scale financial tracking systems * Good understanding and practical knowledge in cloud based services (e.g. gRPC, GraphQL, Docker/Kubernetes, cloud services such as AWS, etc.) In\-office expectations Office\-assigned Stripes in most of our locations are currently expected to spend at least 50% of the time in a given month in their local office or with users. This expectation may vary depending on role, team and location. For example, Stripes in Stripe Delivery Center roles in Mexico City, Mexico and Bengaluru, India work 100% from the office. Also, some teams have greater in\-office attendance requirements, to appropriately support our users and workflows, which the hiring manager will discuss. This approach helps strike a balance between bringing people together for in\-person collaboration and learning from each other, while supporting flexibility when possible. Pay and benefits The annual salary range for this role in the primary location is €85,000 \- €159,600\. This range may change if you are hired in another location. For sales roles, the range provided is the role’s On Target Earnings (“OTE”) range, meaning that the range includes both the sales commissions/sales bonuses target and annual base salary for the role. This salary range may be inclusive of several career levels at Stripe and will be narrowed during the interview process based on a number of factors, including the candidate’s experience, qualifications, and specific location. Applicants interested in this role and who are not located in the primary location may request the annual salary range for their location during the interview process. Specific benefits and details about what compensation is included in the salary range listed above will vary depending on the applicant’s location and can be discussed in more detail during the interview process. Benefits/additional compensation for this role may include: equity, company bonus or sales commissions/bonuses; retirement plans; health benefits; and wellness stipends.

title: Calypso Analyst
description: **Company Description** Kryptos Technologies Limited, headquartered in London, United Kingdom, is a global leader in technology, consulting, and professional IT services, catering to diverse industry domains such as Banking, Insurance, Telecom, Retail, and Energy. The company offers a wide array of services, including strategic consulting, outsourcing, managed services, and cloud solutions, tailored to meet the evolving needs of its clients. Kryptos is renowned for its innovative approach, dedication to client success, and adaptability to emerging market opportunities. With a commitment to excellence, innovation, and professionalism, Kryptos delivers impactful solutions across the globe. **Role Description** This is a contract role for a Calypso Analyst, based on\-site in the London Area, United Kingdom. The Calypso Analyst will be responsible for analyzing, configuring, and implementing Calypso solutions to meet client needs. They will collaborate with cross\-functional teams, ensure seamless system integration, troubleshoot issues, and provide technical support. Additional duties include gathering and documenting requirements, developing workflows, and staying current with Calypso upgrades and functionalities. **Qualifications** * Strong expertise in Calypso implementation, configuration, and development * Understanding of financial instruments, trading systems, and risk management frameworks * Proficient in programming and scripting languages relevant to Calypso, such as Java or SQL * Experience with system integration and workflow customization * Strong problem\-solving skills with a focus on troubleshooting and debugging * Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills * Ability to collaborate with cross\-functional teams and work in an on\-site environment * Bachelor's or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Finance, or related field * Experience in similar projects or financial domains is an advantage

title: Change Analyst
description: **Joining Arup** Arup’s purpose, shared values and collaborative approach has set us apart for over 75 years, guiding how we shape a better world. Arup’s Digital Technology group is home to our Service Management Excellence team, a collaborative and forward‑thinking group that drives best practice, service quality and continual improvement across our global technology landscape. We help shape the way Arup designs, delivers and operates its digital services, ensuring they are reliable, well‑governed and continually evolving. As part of this team, you’ll contribute to strengthening our Change Enablement practice, enabling safer, more predictable and more efficient change across our services. **The Opportunity** At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective – in which we encourage individuality to thrive. Our strength comes from how we respect, share and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives and ideas. You will have the opportunity do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, to our members and to the clients and communities we serve. * As a Change Enablement Specialist, you’ll play a central role in coordinating, assessing and supporting the delivery of changes across Arup’s global digital environment. You will manage Change Requests end‑to‑end, ensuring they are well‑assessed, well‑communicated and safely implemented. You will also chair CAB/ECAB meetings, maintain high‑quality change data and collaborate closely with technical teams to ensure changes land effectively and with minimal risk. * This role offers an excellent opportunity to build your expertise in IT service management while contributing to tangible improvements in service stability and digital delivery. What’s unique about this role — what’s in it for you? * You will be the operational owner of the Change Enablement lifecycle, coordinating changes that directly support Arup’s global digital operations. * You will chair CAB and ECAB sessions, developing confidence and capability as a facilitator and trusted advisor. * You will work closely with engineering, application and operational teams, providing broad exposure to diverse technologies and service landscapes. * You will contribute to improvement initiatives that refine our processes, automation and data quality — allowing you to shape how we work. * You’ll join a supportive practice where knowledge\-sharing, collaboration and professional growth are prioritised. Growth and development opportunities * Build deep expertise in ITIL 4 Change Enablement and ServiceNow Change Management. * Gain exposure to impact assessments, risk evaluation, and leading structured decision\-making forums. * Collaborate with our Senior Service Configuration \& Change Specialist and Service Configuration Specialist, broadening your understanding of CMDB, CSDM, and service mapping. * Contribute to enhancements in automation, workflow design, and Change governance. * Access Arup’s learning resources, mentoring and global communities to grow your career in ITSM. Key responsibilities * Manage and coordinate all Change Requests through the full lifecycle, ensuring accuracy, risk awareness and quality documentation. * Review submissions and engage with stakeholders to ensure changes are well‑assessed and ready for implementation. * Chair or facilitate CAB/ECAB meetings, ensuring effective preparation, discussion and follow‑up. * Maintain the Forward Schedule of Change, ensuring visibility and coordination of upcoming work. * Maintain high‑quality, audit‑ready change records and contribute to change performance and trend reporting. * Collaborate with technical teams to understand impacts, dependencies and readiness for change. * Contribute to practice improvements such as workflow optimisation, automation or reporting enhancements. * Support integration between Change Enablement and Configuration Management by ensuring change data aligns with CI/service data. * Assist with basic CMDB/CSDM activities when needed (e.g., CI data validation, service mapping checks). What a typical day might look like * Reviewing new Change submissions, assessing quality, and coordinating with technical teams for clarifications or updates. * Preparing the CAB agenda, chairing a Virtual CAB session and recording decisions and required actions. * Updating the FSC, validating dependencies or scheduling considerations and communicating changes to stakeholders. * Investigating a failed or emergency change, helping to coordinate corrective actions and documenting insights. * Working with the Senior Specialist on an improvement initiative, such as refining risk scoring or testing workflow enhancements in ServiceNow. * Collaborating with the Service Configuration Specialist to cross‑check CI data associated with upcoming changes. **Did you know:** Our Digital Technology team are proud prior winners of the IT Team of the Year Award at the Chartered Institute of IT’s UK IT Industry Awards. **Is this role right for you?** * Experience in Change Enablement, with confidence managing Change Requests throughout their lifecycle. * Ability to chair CAB/ECAB meetings, facilitating constructive discussions and supporting informed decisions. * Hands‑on familiarity with ServiceNow, including Change workflows and reporting. * Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, able to guide discussions and explain impacts clearly. * A detail‑focused, organised and improvement‑oriented mindset, with a desire to enhance process maturity. **What We Offer You** At Arup, we care about each member’s success, so we can grow together. Guided by our values, we provide an attractive total reward package that recognises the contribution of each of our members to our shared success. As well as competitive, fair and equitable pay, we offer a career in which all of our members can belong, grow and thrive – through benefits that support health and wellbeing, a wide range of learning opportunities and many possibilities to have an impact through the work they do. We are owned in trust on behalf of our members, giving us the freedom, with personal responsibility, to set our own direction and choose work that aligns with our purpose and adds to Arup’s legacy. Our members collaborate on ambitious projects to deliver remarkable outcomes for our clients and communities. Profit Share is a key part of our reward, enabling members to share in the results of our collective efforts. **Different people, shared values** Arup is an equal opportunity employer that actively promotes and nurtures a diverse and inclusive workforce. We welcome applications from individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, marital status, disability, neurotype or mental health, race or ethnicity, faith or belief, sexual orientation, socioeconomic background, and whether you’re pregnant or on family leave. We are an open environment that embraces diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas – this drives our excellence. Guided by our values and alignment with the UN Sustainable Development Goals, we create and contribute to equitable spaces and systems, while cultivating a sense of belonging for all. Our internal employee networks support our inclusive culture: from race, ethnicity and cross\-cultural working to gender equity and LGBTQ\+ and disability inclusion – we aim to create a space for you to express yourself and make a positive difference. Discover more about life at Arup at Careers \- Your Life at Arup . We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible to all candidates. Please let us know if you need any assistance or reasonable adjustments throughout your application or interview process, and/or to perform the essential functions of the role. We will do everything we can to support you. **Our Application Process** To understand what to expect next, please visit our Careers page Our aim is to make this process as streamlined and easy as possible for our applicants. Please note that all applicants must apply directly via the job portal and applications sent via email will not be considered. If this role is not quite what you are looking for, but you are interested in other opportunities for a future with purpose, please sign up to our Talent Community where you will be kept up to date with roles suitable for you to shape a better world. Stay safe online \- Arup will never ask for payment or your bank details as part of our recruitment process.

title: Chartered Accountant
description: A well\-established, partner\-led accountancy practice in North Dublin is seeking a **Chartered Accountant** to join its growing team. This is an excellent opportunity offering exposure to prestigious clients, flexible work/life balance, and fast\-track career progression. The successful candidate can expect promotion after 12 months, with a clear career path to **Director** and ultimately **Partner** level. This role suits an ambitious professional seeking long\-term career development within a supportive and dynamic firm environment. **Responsibilities** * Manage a portfolio of clients, building and maintaining strong professional relationships. * Work across a diverse range of assignments including **audit, accounts preparation, corporate and personal tax, and advisory services** . * Provide high\-quality advice and service to clients in line with best practice and firm standards. * Supervise junior team members and contribute to their development where appropriate. * Collaborate closely with partners and directors to deliver client\-focused solutions. **Requirements** * **ACA/ACCA/CTA Qualified Accountant** . * Practice\-trained and qualified with a **minimum 2 years’ Irish experience** (0–5 years PQE considered). * Proven experience across **audit, accounts preparation, and tax** . * Proficiency with relevant **accounting and tax software packages** . * Excellent **communication, interpersonal, and organisational skills** . * Ability to **manage multiple priorities** and meet deadlines effectively. **Salary \& Benefits** * **€54,000 – €68,000** (commensurate with experience). * **Pension contributions** * **Annual bonus** * **Comprehensive benefits package** and flexible working hours. * **Promotion expected after 12 months** , with a transparent career pathway to Partner level. * Supportive, friendly, and collaborative working environment. * Ongoing **training, mentorship, and professional development resources** . * On site parking **Interested?** If you’re an experienced **Chartered Accountant** looking for a new challenge with a firm that values quality, autonomy, and career growth—or if you’d like to have a confidential conversation—contact **Andrew** at **086 191 9057** or andrew **@gpcfinance.com** . At GPC Finance, we’ll always speak with you first before sharing your details with any client. All conversations are held in the strictest confidence.

title: Civil and Structural Engineering Graduates
description: Iarnród Éireann Graduate Programme 2026 Your Journey Starts Here….. ### **Civil and Structural Engineering graduates** At Iarnród Éireann, we’re driving a once\-in\-a\-generation transformation—positioning rail at the heart of Ireland’s sustainable transport future through the Irish Rail Strategy 2050\. Our people are central to delivering this ambitious vision—across modern infrastructure upgrades, next\-generation fleet development, customer experience innovation, and strategic port connections. We are now seeking passionate graduates to become part of our ‘One Team’—supporting the delivery of safe, reliable, and future\-ready rail services nationwide. This is your chance to be part of a dynamic team building a greener, smarter, and more connected Ireland. **We are looking for ambitious and innovative Civil and Structural Engineering graduates who have achieved or are working towards a Level 8 Honours Degree.** **The Iarnród Éireann Irish Rail Graduate Programme** Iarnród Éireann Irish Rail Graduate Programme is a multi\-award\-winning programme in recognition of the innovative and multi\-faced elements in the programme, culminating in a defined career pathway for each professional discipline. **Our Award Winning Programme:** * provides a structured orientation programme to meet and gain valuable knowledge from senior management * offers graduates an opportunity to work in all aspects of the professional discipline they are applying for * includes a specifically designed bespoke graduate programme for the individual * each programme contains placements to ensure a rounded and multi\-faceted experience * support provided by experienced mentors for the entire duration of the programme * provides support through our Occupational Health and Wellbeing Services * provides opportunities for growth and excellent career paths for those who stand out and deliver **Our Rewards Package includes:** * A competitive salary * Laptop and mobile phone * Mentoring \& support * Continuous Professional Development (CPD) * Cycle to Work Scheme * Health and Wellness Programme * Reduced fares on the network * Professional membership Please email your CV and Cover letter to**GraduateProgramme@irishrail.ie** **with “Civil \& Structural Engineering Graduate Programme 2026”** included in the subject line. Closing date for applications is **Monday 6th April.**

title: Client Service Executive

Requirements of this **Client Service Executive** Job: * A minimum of 1 year experience working in an administrative position within financial services * Degree or Masters level in Business/Finance related area * A high level of proficiency in MS Work, Excel, Outlook and PowerPoint * Excellent attention to detail * Excellent organisational skills This is an excellent opportunity for someone who is looking to kickstart their career in the Wealth Management space. title: Consultant Psychiatrist Intellectual Disabilities
description: We are looking for a Consultant Psychiatrist in Intellectual Disabilities to work for our client, a leading provider of residential care for individuals with Intellectual Disabilities and Mental Health conditions in Ireland. The Consultant Psychiatrist in Intellectual Disabilities provides specialist psychiatric assessment, diagnosis, and treatment to children and adolescents with moderate to profound intellectual disabilities and co‑occurring moderate to severe mental health disorders. The role forms part of a multidisciplinary team (MDT) and contributes to high‑quality, person‑centred mental health care. **Key Responsibilities** * Deliver comprehensive psychiatric assessments, diagnosis, and treatment planning for mental health disorders in young people with intellectual disabilities, including anxiety, depression, psychosis, ADHD/hyperkinesis, challenging behaviour, and comorbid ASD‑related mental health issues. * Provide ongoing clinical management, including medication oversight, risk assessment, and crisis intervention. * Work collaboratively with MDT members (psychologists, nurses, behaviour specialists, SLTs, OTs, social workers) to develop integrated care plans. * Support families, schools, and disability services through consultation, psychoeducation, and coordinated care planning. * Participate in service development, clinical governance, audits, and quality improvement initiatives. * Provide clinical leadership, supervision, and guidance to junior medical staff, nurse prescribers, and other healthcare professionals. * Maintain accurate clinical documentation and prepare reports when required (e.g., for care planning or court proceedings). **Requirements** * Specialist registration with the Irish Medical Council in Psychiatry. * Significant experience in child and adolescent mental health and/or intellectual disability psychiatry. * Strong skills in multidisciplinary collaboration, clinical decision‑making, and managing complex presentations. * Commitment to values‑based, person‑centred care for individuals with intellectual disabilities and complex needs. **What is on Offer:** * Competitive Salary * Benefits Package * Professional development

title: Data Analyst
description: We are looking for a talented Data Analyst with 2–4 years’ experience to join our growing team. In this client\-facing role, you will work closely with engineers, project managers, and other stakeholders to gather and analyse data from a variety of project sources. Your insights will help our teams and clients make data\-driven decisions, improve workflows, and identify trends that reduce risk and enhance project performance. We pride ourselves on being innovative and at the cutting edge of digital construction, particularly in the areas of scheduling, reporting, and visualisation. We are keen that our Data Analyst has the same drive and determination to innovate. As part of a dynamic team, you will use tools such as Power BI, Excel, and SQL to develop and maintain reporting dashboards and visualisations. You’ll also work with Python (or similar programming languages), build and manage data pipelines, contribute to Power Platform and internal app development, and support internal and external ML/AI initiatives. You’ll join a collaborative, innovative, and supportive team environment with opportunities to develop your skills and grow within the company, while working on cutting\-edge data analytics projects across the construction and engineering sectors. **Key Responsibilities:** * Work with internal teams and clients to understand reporting needs and translate them into clear data solutions. * Gather, clean, combine and validate data from multiple project sources to ensure it is accurate, consistent and ready for analysis. * Design and build Power BI dashboards and reports that clearly communicate project performance and trends to both technical and non\-technical stakeholders. * Use Excel and SQL for data exploration, analysis, and troubleshooting. * Help to create and maintain data pipelines (e.g. scheduled data refreshes, automated data flows) to keep reports up to date. * Support development of solutions using the Microsoft Power Platform (e.g. Power Apps, Power Automate) to streamline workflows and data capture. * Use basic programming skills (ideally Python) to automate repetitive tasks, transform data, or integrate with APIs where needed. * Work as part of a project team, contributing to planning, documentation, and quality checks on data and reports. * Communicate findings clearly in meetings, presentations and written summaries, highlighting risks, trends and recommendations. * Stay up to date with good practices in data analytics, reporting and the Microsoft ecosystem (Power BI, Power Platform, Azure, etc.). **Qualifications / Experience:** Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field (or equivalent experience) with 2–4 years in a data analyst or similar role. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills, with attention to detail and good communication for explaining insights to non\-technical stakeholders. * Proficient in Power BI (data modelling, visuals, dashboards) and good working knowledge of Excel. * Practical experience with SQL and some programming (preferably Python) for data transformation and automation. * Exposure to data pipelines/ETL and ideally Microsoft Power Platform, with ability to work in teams and client\-facing environments. * Experience in construction, engineering or technical services desirable but not essential * Additional beneficial skills include basic front\-end development (e.g., Power Apps or simple web reporting), familiarity with data warehousing/APIs/cloud platforms (e.g., Azure), and interest or exposure to predictive analytics or basic machine learning. * Must have the right to work full time in the EU. **Diversity, Equality \& Inclusion:** At Strata, we believe that diversity drives innovation and inclusion fuels success. We are committed to creating a workplace where everyone feels valued, respected, and empowered to thrive. We welcome applications from candidates of all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives, and we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. Please send your C.V. to careers@stratadigital.io

title: Development Economist
description: **Development Economist (AI Training)** **About The Role** We’re seeking development economists to help improve AI systems trained on global and development economics. * Organization: Alignerr (Powered by Labelbox) * Location: Remote * Type: Hourly / Task\-based Contract * Compensation: $35\-70 /hour * Commitment: 10\-40 hours/week **What You’ll Do** * Review development economics questions and explanations used in AI training. * Assess reasoning related to growth, inequality, and global development. * Provide structured feedback to improve accuracy and contextual nuance. * Apply real\-world or academic experience to validate scenarios. * Work independently and asynchronously to meet project deadlines. **Who You Are** * 3\+ years of experience teaching or researching development economics. * Strong understanding of global economic issues and data interpretation. * Able to evaluate written economic reasoning clearly and critically. * Comfortable working with structured review tasks. **Nice to Have** * Graduate degree in Economics or related field. * Field, NGO, or international policy experience. * Familiarity with AI or content evaluation workflows. **Why Join Us** * Competitive pay and flexible remote work. * Lead a team working on cutting\-edge AI projects. * Exposure to advanced LLMs and how they’re trained. * Freelance perks: autonomy, flexibility, and global collaboration. * Potential for contract extension. **Application Process (Takes 10\-15 min)** * Submit your resume * Complete a short screening * Project matching and onboarding *PS: Our team reviews applications daily. Please complete your AI interview and application steps to be considered for this opportunity.*

title: Domain Architect Security
description: Because we strive to put people first. Culture, our way. Technology at Primark What’s a career at Primark all about? The positive impact you’re making, the experiences you’re having and the people you’re with. You’re our inspiration. Embrace what sets you apart, own your career and develop in ways you never expected. Leave your mark. And do it your way. Our technology team is actively shaping the next wave of advancements. Engaged with innovative initiatives, your expertise will propel our business into the future. Collaborating with a creative team of tech enthusiasts, you’ll contribute your unique skills to fuel our technological advancements. **What You’ll Do as** **Domain Architect Security** In your role, you’ll collaborate with different people across a range of skillsets. Here’s a flavour of your day\-to\-day: * Provide strategic architectural leadership by defining the vision, roadmap, and multi‑year planning for the domain. * Evaluate and shape strategic solution options, ensuring alignment across systems, integrations, data, and processes. * Lead domain governance by running design forums, assuring high‑level designs, and enforcing enterprise architecture standards. * Collaborate across Cyber, Product, Platform, and Programme teams to shape demand, sequencing, and solution options. * Drive delivery excellence through vendor selection support, PoCs, cross‑domain alignment, and architectural oversight. * Mentor architects and engineers while maintaining domain knowledge, documentation accuracy, and contributing to practice development. **What You’ll Get** People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle: Let’s talk lifestyle: Healthcare, pension, and potential bonus. 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. Because Primark is all about tailoring to you, we offer Tax Saver Tickets, fitness centre, and a subsidised cafeteria. **What You’ll Bring** Here at Primark, we want everyone to feel valued – so please bring your authentic self to work, of course with some other key experience and abilities for this role in particular: * Deep enterprise and security architecture expertise, including frameworks such as TOGAF, SABSA, OSA, plus strong strategic thinking, risk\-based decision\-making, and organisational capability. * Strong stakeholder, communication, and collaboration ability, translating complex security concepts into simple language and driving alignment across business, product, and technology teams. * Proven architectural leadership, with 10\+ years designing and delivering large‑scale, secure\-by\-design solutions and guiding major programmes, junior peers, and technical teams. * Broad technical security knowledge, covering IAM, network, cloud (Azure/AWS/GCP), data protection, threat management, plus role‑dependent strengths in areas like CSPM, DLP, encryption, SIEM/SOC, and network segmentation. * Strong governance and compliance experience, applying frameworks such as ISO 27001, NIST, CIS Controls, alongside GDPR, PCI DSS, and risk management methodologies. * Additional desirable capabilities, including Zero Trust, secure API design, DevSecOps, retail\-sector experience, and familiarity with fashion/retail technology environments. Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future \- and we’re excited to develop yours. **About Primark** At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business and over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility request, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist. All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns, please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss. Our fashion isn’t one\-size\-fits\-all and neither is our culture. Primark promotes equal employment opportunity, we strive to create an inclusive workplace where people can be themselves, access opportunities and thrive together. **REQ ID:** JR\-4225

title: Dutch Billing Associate
description: Before you apply to a job, select your language preference from the options available at the top right of this page. Explore your next opportunity at a Fortune Global 500 organization. How we recruit At UPS, equal opportunities, fair treatment and an inclusive workplace environment are of utmost importance. Every day, we manage the flow of goods, funds, and information in more than 200 countries and territories worldwide. Come join us! **Employee Type:** Permanent UPS is committed to providing a workplace free of discrimination, harassment, and retaliation. **Employee Type:** Permanent UPS is committed to providing a workplace free of discrimination, harassment, and retaliation.

title: ESB's Civil Engineering Technician (CAD) Apprenticeship Programme 2026
description: Vacancy Number: 28123 Category: Apprentice \- Business Job Type: Temporary Full\-time **ESB’s Civil Engineering Technician (CAD) Apprenticeship Programme 2026** Are you looking for a career in engineering in the energy sector? Would you like to become a civil engineering technician, specialising in CAD, learning in a hybrid college/workplace environment, earning while you learn? If you do, why not join ESB to complete your Certificate in Civil Engineering specialising in CAD by apprenticeship? We are Ireland’s foremost energy company, where we are driven to make a difference, to achieving Net Zero by 2040! With over 1000 engineers and related professionals, ESB is dedicated to investing in engineering related expertise and developing critical skills to support Ireland’s future growth and development. We see investing in our people, the current and future drivers of our organisation, as a central pillar of our strategy. Our Civil Engineering CAD Apprenticeship Programme is aimed at school leavers, or others who want to change their career path, who have an interest in engineering and would prefer an apprenticeship approach to completing their education. The objective of our Programme is to launch you on a track to career success. You will grow professionally, develop key skills, and actively contribute to ESB’s Net Zero by 2040 strategy while also gaining a certificate in Civil engineering specialising in CAD over two years. The programme will involve: * Formal attendance (in\-person and virtually) at either in ATU Galway or ATU Sligo (depending on college allocated to ESB) as per Certificate in Civil Engineering specialising in CAD brochure * Full time on\-the\-job training/work when not studying. On\-the\-job training will be related to course modules as well as providing broader experience. It will involve being based at ESB’s offices in ‘The Sidings’ Grand Canal Dock with time on site and other locations as and when required. * Hybrid working is a feature of the engineering work in ESB, but it is expected that during training a significant amount of attendance in the office and some on\-site attendance will be required. * Mentoring to support you in both your formal and on\-the\-job studies. * An ESB assessment near the end of your programme for access to a permanent CAD technician role in one of our civil engineering departments. Our Entry Requirements: In addition to the minimum entry requirements outlined by the college, ESB is looking for people with the following qualifications: * An O6/H7 or higher in leaving cert mathematics. * An O6/H7 or higher in the leaving cert (or equivalent) in: * Irish / English, * At least one of Chemistry, Physics, Physics \& Chemistry, Construction Studies, Design and Communication, Graphics, Engineering, Technical Drawing and Technology, * Any 2 other subjects. * A full drivers licence valid for driving for work in Ireland. In addition: As an employee on our Civil Engineering CAD Apprenticeship Programme, you will enjoy our many benefits including a competitive salary (with annual increments on successful completion of each academic year) and a comprehensive benefits package built on the four pillars of reward, career, community, and wellbeing. Location ESB’s offices in ‘The Sidings’ Grand Canal Dock What We Look For? * A proven ability to work as part of a team. * An ability to juggle tasks and manage one’s own workload. * A thirst for continuous, independent learning. * A keen interest in delivering the net zero, resilient, reliable electricity infrastructure which Ireland needs. * An alignment with the ESB core values – we are Courageous, Caring, Driven and Trusted. Applications close on 13th April 2026\. Places will be offered subject to meeting the minimum college entry requirements before the end of July 2026 **Diversity, Equity and Inclusion Statement** ESB is committed to being an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from all sections of society and ensure that no one is discriminated against on the grounds of race, religion or belief, ethnicity or nationality, disability, age, citizenship, marital status, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation or gender identity, or any other basis as protected by law. Applicants who anticipate requiring assistance or reasonable accommodations for any part of the application or interview process may contact, in confidence, recruitment@esb.ie. **About ESB** Join us in our mission to achieve a net\-zero electricity system by 2040\. ESB is Ireland’s leading energy utility, with activities spanning electricity generation, transmission and distribution, energy supply, energy services and international consultancy. Operating in Ireland, Northern Ireland and Great Britain, we invest over €1 billion each year to deliver a new energy future based on reliable, affordable zero\-carbon electricity.

title: ETF Change Management - Vice President
description: **About This Role** **About BlackRock:** Elevate your career by joining the world's largest asset manager! Would you thrive in an environment that fosters positive relationships and recognises outstanding performance? We value and recognise your contributions daily. At BlackRock, we strive to empower our employees and effectively engage your involvement in our success. BlackRock is one of the world’s preeminent asset management firms and a premier provider of global investment management, risk management and advisory services to institutional, intermediary, and individual investors around the world. With over $13 trillion in assets, we have an outstanding responsibility: our technology and services empower millions of investors to save for retirement, pay for college, buy a home and improve their financial well\-being. BlackRock is the \#1 Exchange Traded Fund (ETF) provider globally with over $5 trillion in assets under management. With more than two decades of ETF innovation and expertise behind us, we serve investors of all sorts, all around the world. We offer clients a comprehensive range of products that provide transparent and cost\-efficient access to a full range of asset classes and global markets. **Your Team** The EPIC Team (ETF Platform Innovation and Change) is a group of change experts within Technology \& Operations that drive strategy, implementation, and transformation projects central to our global ETF growth objectives. We represent a unified platform team that connects the firm and platform horizontally to achieve best\-in\-class ETF operating models and results for our clients. We are a versatile team composed of individuals with diverse experiences and backgrounds who enjoy the opportunity of leading high impact cross\-functional projects across our fastest\-growing ETF products, optimizing processes, and using technology to increase efficiency and reduce complexity across the ETF ecosystem. In order to accomplish its mission, EPIC puts great emphasis on developing its people, enhancing their leadership and delivery skills and expanding their firm and industry knowledge. **About This Role** You will gain an understanding of how the firm and ETF ecosystem functions and leverage your financial markets / investment management industry knowledge and your technical abilities to collaborate with business, technology, product and operational leaders in delivering the firm’s most critical and valuable ETF projects. This is not a typical change management role where you only handle project coordination / facilitation. We are looking for top\-level problem solvers who like getting into the details and are actively engaged (i.e., roll up your sleeves) in creating and implementing scalable solutions that usually involve process, technology, and organizational change. You can expect a significant, varied role that will help ensure BlackRock’s businesses have the necessary capabilities to support an aggressive growth agenda. A successful candidate will have experience working across functions, setting and driving a strategic agenda, being involved in high profile projects, and achieving positive results through diligent and relentless execution **Responsibilities** * Design, innovate, and implement ETF operating models, technology solutions and commercial opportunities * Operate horizontally at the intersection of capital markets, investments, ETF servicing, post trade processing, technology, and core services * Lead and drive ETF platform change both internally at BlackRock and externally across Service Providers partnering with business leaders * Become the “go\-to” platform specialists connecting the ETF Ecosystem, ETF Solutions Platform (ESP) \& Aladdin * Develop a solutions capability for tactical and strategic needs referencing API’s and data across the ETF and Investment book for record * Partner with the firm’s leadership to help shape BlackRock’s ETF platform strategy to maximize growth, scale, and consistency * Develop project objectives with measurable goals, drive consensus and communicate plans and results to stakeholders and management throughout * Establish, oversee and execute complex multi\-faceted testing strategies to deliver change * Maintain a One BlackRock perspective driving positive and impactful outcomes for the firm * Mentor junior team members and contribute to the team’s overall development. * Simplify complex topics and create innovative solutions for issues * Engage and maintains relationships with stakeholders across the firm to effectively navigate our global organization **Qualifications** * 5\-7\+ years of experience in financial services or a related industry, including experience engaging with leadership executives and successfully driving and delivering complex and impactful strategic change programs * A background that includes exposure to ETF operations, client service, fund accounting, custody/banking * Ability to define holistic business requirements and communicate the needs of the team effectively * Experience in platform design and system integration * Outstanding leadership experience with a track record of successful people management outcomes * Detailed knowledge and understanding of investment management products and asset types, including but not limited to ETFs, Mutual Funds, Fixed Income, Equities, Derivatives, and Alternatives. * Significant and successful track record in driving and delivering complex and impactful strategic change programs * Experience navigating a complex matrix of accountability and stakeholders * Excellent written and verbal communication skills: ability to clearly articulate objectives and influence outcomes * Results driven individual with a high level of attention to detail and intellectual curiosity * Ability to manage multiple complex projects at the same time * Technical aptitude, understanding how technology drives scale and the ability to sit at the intersection of business subject matter experts, product management and engineers * Ability to gather and analyze facts, analyze big data, define problems, and suggest solutions * Prior experience or interest in data analysis including python, sql, API’s and power bi **Our Benefits** To help you stay energized, engaged and inspired, we offer a wide range of employee benefits including: retirement investment and tools designed to help you in building a sound financial future; access to education reimbursement; comprehensive resources to support your physical health and emotional well\-being; family support programs; and Flexible Time Off (FTO) so you can relax, recharge and be there for the people you care about. **Our hybrid work model** BlackRock’s hybrid work model is designed to enable a culture of collaboration and apprenticeship that enriches the experience of our employees, while supporting flexibility for all. Employees are currently required to work at least 4 days in the office per week, with the flexibility to work from home 1 day a week. Some business groups may require more time in the office due to their roles and responsibilities. We remain focused on increasing the impactful moments that arise when we work together in person – aligned with our commitment to performance and innovation. As a new joiner, you can count on this hybrid model to accelerate your learning and onboarding experience here at BlackRock. **About BlackRock** At BlackRock, we are all connected by one mission: to help more and more people experience financial well\-being. Our clients, and the people they serve, are saving for retirement, paying for their children’s educations, buying homes and starting businesses. Their investments also help to strengthen the global economy: support businesses small and large; finance infrastructure projects that connect and power cities; and facilitate innovations that drive progress. This mission would not be possible without our smartest investment – the one we make in our employees. It’s why we’re dedicated to creating an environment where our colleagues feel welcomed, valued and supported with networks, benefits and development opportunities to help them thrive. For additional information on BlackRock, please visit @blackrock \| Twitter: @blackrock \| LinkedIn: www.linkedin.com/company/blackrock BlackRock is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We evaluate qualified applicants without regard to age, disability, race, religion, sex, sexual orientation and other protected characteristics at law.

title: FP&A Analyst
description: **We are partnering with a major national energy organisation to appoint an FP\&A Analyst. This is a key business‑facing role that blends financial modelling, forecasting, performance analysis, and cross‑functional stakeholder engagement.** **The position offers strong exposure to senior management, ownership of budgeting and planning cycles, and the opportunity to influence decision‑making through high‑quality financial insight and commentary.** **Responsibilities:** * Leading the budgeting, forecasting, and long‑term planning processes, ensuring timely and accurate submissions. * Partnering with business units to understand financial drivers, identify risks and opportunities, and support commercial decision‑making. * Analysing monthly performance, preparing clear variance analysis, insights, and presentations for senior stakeholders. * Developing and enhancing financial models, dashboards, and analytical tools to improve visibility and accuracy. * Driving continuous improvement and supporting automation across FP\&A processes. * Ensuring strong governance, control compliance, and alignment with audit standards. **Requirements:** * Qualified accountant (ACA, ACCA, CIMA) with at least 2 years’ post‑qualification experience. * Excellent financial, analytical, and problem‑solving skills. * Strong Excel capability with experience in data‑driven analysis; Power BI or similar tools an advantage. * Confident communicator with the ability to engage and challenge senior stakeholders effectively. * Highly organised, detail‑oriented, and comfortable managing competing priorities. * Experience with budgeting, forecasting, or financial modelling beneficial; familiarity with SAP or other planning systems a plus. **Our client offers a hybrid working environment (minimum two days on‑site), flexible working arrangements, and the opportunity to contribute to Ireland’s transition to a net‑zero energy system.** Apply directly via the link provided or contact Matthew Fleming at mfleming@elevatepartners.ie.
