title: Financial Data Analyst, Technical Operations
description: Who we are **About Stripe** Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies \- from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups \- use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone's reach while doing the most important work of your career. **About The Team** The Payments organization owns some of Stripe’s most critical payment flows and a platform that processes hundreds of billions of dollars in payments a year. Our team is responsible for translating complex partner specifications related to network costs (interchange and scheme fees) into simplified logic for internal and external consumption. This also drives decisions and product recommendations to manage the underlying network costs paid by Stripe and our users. The team partners closely with the engineering, product, finance, and partnership groups to manage and understand Stripe’s network costs. Our work is core to Stripe’s business, as Technical Operations roles in Payments are a dynamic and key component of Stripe's success. We sit at the intersection of product/platform engineers and financial partners, connecting them to ensure that everyone thrives and nothing is lost in translation. What you’ll do We are looking to add payment enthusiasts who enjoy interpreting complex cost structures and ever changing payment network systems in order to optimize on behalf of Stripe and our users. You will be instrumental in building Stripe’s approach to managing our global network cost base. **Responsibilities** * Collaborate across the company, including engineering, accounting, financial partnerships and product teams, to analyze billions of dollars moving through the Stripe platform. * Translate network specifications into implementation instructions to create billing models for new and revised fees (both scheme and interchange) * Influence product strategy to minimize network costs * Work with Product and Engineering to implement cost saving opportunities * Partner with Finance to prioritize areas for investment and explain trends in costs and margin impact * Monitor and maintain the accuracy of the fees through validation and alerting * Creating fee explainability to internal and external stakeholders * Hosting education sessions for internal teams to scale knowledge * Introduce data and process efficiencies to meet the scale and speed of Stripe. Identify and automate inefficient operational processes to reduce manual workloads * Scale our network cost operation by driving the design, documentation, and training of operational processes to leverage partner relationships. Who you are We’re looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. **Minimum Requirements** * Have 5\+ years of in Payments at a leading technology or payments company * Have experience writing SQL to query relational databases * Have experience managing communications and workflows with technical and non\-technical teams across internal and external stakeholders * Experience mentoring junior team members in technical skill development, operational strategy and problem solving * Have experience navigating the nuanced complexity of financial systems and solving large scale, technical challenges * Have strong financial modeling and analytical skills to investigate, prioritize, and identify the root cause of issues * Have experience managing multiple, concurrent work streams, identifying and triaging technical issues and driving solutions with clear metrics to show impact In\-office expectations Office\-assigned Stripes in most of our locations are currently expected to spend at least 50% of the time in a given month in their local office or with users. This expectation may vary depending on role, team and location. For example, Stripes in Stripe Delivery Center roles in Mexico City, Mexico and Bengaluru, India work 100% from the office. Also, some teams have greater in\-office attendance requirements, to appropriately support our users and workflows, which the hiring manager will discuss. This approach helps strike a balance between bringing people together for in\-person collaboration and learning from each other, while supporting flexibility when possible. Pay and benefits The annual salary range for this role in the primary location is €84,000 \- €126,000\. This range may change if you are hired in another location. For sales roles, the range provided is the role’s On Target Earnings (“OTE”) range, meaning that the range includes both the sales commissions/sales bonuses target and annual base salary for the role. This salary range may be inclusive of several career levels at Stripe and will be narrowed during the interview process based on a number of factors, including the candidate’s experience, qualifications, and specific location. Applicants interested in this role and who are not located in the primary location may request the annual salary range for their location during the interview process. Specific benefits and details about what compensation is included in the salary range listed above will vary depending on the applicant’s location and can be discussed in more detail during the interview process. Benefits/additional compensation for this role may include: equity, company bonus or sales commissions/bonuses; retirement plans; health benefits; and wellness stipends.

title: German Billing Associate
description: Before you apply to a job, select your language preference from the options available at the top right of this page. Explore your next opportunity at a Fortune Global 500 organization. Envision innovative possibilities, experience our rewarding culture, and work with talented teams that help you become better every day. We know what it takes to lead UPS into tomorrow—people with a unique combination of skill passion. If you have the qualities and drive to lead yourself or teams, there are roles ready to cultivate your skills and take you to the next level. **Job Description:** FUTURE YOU supports customers and commercial integrity Are you a natural when it comes to getting customers on your side? Do you also possess an interest in finance? If the answer to both these questions is yes, and you also have a talent for languages, you could be the candidate we’ve been searching for. We need you to secure the loyalty of our customers by showing them how swiftly we can resolve their billing issues. You’ll be making a vital commercial impact on the business, and you’ll be doing it while speaking English and German language. What you’ll be doing * Managing billing documents and correcting errors by issuing credit/debit notes. * Raising the bar when it comes to providing customer service on the phone and via email. * Supporting UPS with investigating our systems to prevent future billing inconsistencies. What you’ll get * Competitive salary * Full training to help you perform at your best * Flexible benefits – UPS Pension Scheme/ Healthcare * Life Assurance * Tuition Reimbursement * Opportunities to progress – thanks to our ‘promotion from within policy’. What you’ll need A university education and years of work experience are not essential here. You do need to prove you’ve spent at least one year in customer service, and experience using Microsoft Excel would be an advantage. However, we will provide you with four weeks of initial training on site, followed by a minimum of four weeks integrating you into the office team. Once you’re feeling confident enough to work on your own, you can then submit requests to work remotely until the hybrid work type model is established on the site. How we recruit At UPS, equal opportunities, fair treatment and an inclusive workplace environment are of utmost importance. We are committed to a policy of treating individuals fairly and recruiting, selecting, training, promoting and compensating based on merit, experience and other work–related criteria. We do not discriminate against any applicant based on age, race, religion, sex, disability, sexual orientation or gender identity. A bit about a big business Founded in 1907 as a messenger company in the United States, UPS has grown into a multi\-billion\-dollar corporation by clearly focusing on the goal of enabling commerce around the globe. Today, UPS is a global company with one of the most recognized and admired brands in the world. We have become the worlds largest package delivery company and a leading global provider of specialized transportation and logistics services. Every day, we manage the flow of goods, funds, and information in more than 200 countries and territories worldwide. Come join us! **Employee Type:** Permanent UPS is committed to providing a workplace free of discrimination, harassment, and retaliation. **Employee Type:** Permanent UPS is committed to providing a workplace free of discrimination, harassment, and retaliation.

title: Graduate Engineer
description: **Overview** **Graduate Civil Engineering Opportunities** **2026\- Ireland** John Sisk \& Son are looking for proactive, talented Civil Engineering Graduates to join our 2026 Early Careers Graduate Programme. **The role** Our structured 27\-month graduate programme is designed to give you hands\-on experience across a wide range of construction projects, helping you build the career you want. If you’ve recently graduated or are about to graduate, with a degree in Civil Engineering , and you're ready to take charge of your future with a leading building contractor, we could be the perfect fit. This is your opportunity to make an impact and grow with us. **Who We Are** Established in 1859, Sisk is proud to be in its fifth generation of family ownership. Our founding values of care, excellence, and integrity continue to shape everything we do. With a clear vision of Building Excellence, we consistently deliver high\-quality projects that exceed client expectations. Our people are the driving force behind our success, and we’re now looking for the next generation of graduates to help us carry that legacy forward. **A journey to your future** Our Graduate Programme is a carefully designed 27\-month journey that offers up to three business rotations, giving you broad exposure and the opportunity to shape your career path , fusing technical excellence, commercial acumen, and leadership capabilities. From day one, you'll follow a detailed development plan aligned to a clear timeline, ensuring you gain the experience and competencies required to pursue professional chartership. We’re committed to helping you build a strong technical foundation and excel in your chosen area of specialism. The programme blends structured learning, hands\-on project work, and expert mentoring to deliver both depth and breadth of knowledge. With robust progress tracking, regular feedback, and a dedicated support network, you’ll be empowered to grow with confidence and make a meaningful impact. What We Are Looking For * A collaborative team player with fresh ideas, ready to challenge yourself, and us, every step of the way. With a passion for the built environment and a drive to make a difference, you’ll bring curiosity, resilience, and a commitment to technical excellence. * R ecently graduated or be on track to graduate with a Civil Engineering degree, ready to join Sisk’s Early Careers Graduate Programme in September 2026\. Over 27 months, y ou’ll complete up to three rotations across the business gaining exposure to live projects, commercial operations, and technical delivery. * Geographic mobility is essential \- you’ll be expected to work across the UK, Ireland, and mainland Europe, including at least one rotation outside of your home country. **Additional Information** The selection process has been designed to ensure that we, as a company, select the right individuals that will thrive with us, but also that you, as a candidate, select the right company to build and shape your career with. In return, you will receive a competitive salary, a focused and structured start to your career and its progression in the construction industry, with excellent support and guidance every step of the way. If your application is successful, you will be required to participate in an online assessment. The programme will commence in September 2026 . Closing Date 30th of November 2025 Our people are at the heart of our success and our proposition to them is work with purpose, have time for life, build a career with no limits and surround yourself with great people. At Sisk we are committed to attracting and hiring diverse talent into our organisation. We know the confidence gap and imposter phenomenon can get in the way of meeting remarkable candidates. We also know that candidates from underrepresented backgrounds often don't apply for roles if they don't feel they meet all the criteria. Give your transferable skills the chance to tell the full story. It’s not all about your technical expertise. Don’t hold back, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Graduate Mechanical Engineers 2026
description: **Position** : Graduate Mechanical Engineers 2026 **Location** : Dublin, Kildare, Cork, Limerick, Gavle, Copenhagen, Groningen, Charleroi **Start date:** We operate on a rolling recruitment basis. Please advise your availability to start in your application form. You can discuss this further with your recruiter upon application. **Company Description** We are a leading engineering services company with a proven track record of high quality, customer focused project delivery for over 130 years. Our core divisions include Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Instrumentation, Sprinkler and Fire Protection. Some of the other services we provide include Waste to Energy (BioEnergy), Specialist Lift, Off Site Fabrication, Technical Support Services, Maintenance, Asset Management, BIM (Building Information Modelling) and Geo\-Surveying. We offer competitive salaries, financial support for further training, Continuous Professional Development (CPD) leading to chartered status, performance incentives and the ability to progress your career with a world class engineering contractor. We have a variety of open roles that can vary from Mechanical Engineer, Quality Assurance/Quality Control Engineer, Project Engineer, Site Engineer \& BIM Engineer. **Job Description** * Liaise with suppliers/specialist contractors for procurement of goods and services * Generation of RFIs, technical submittals, equipment registers and trackers * Assist construction team (Supervisor, subcontractor foreman, QAQC personnel) * Liaise with JEG design team, design consultants and construction management team * Prepare handover packs with QA Dept * Track progress of construction programme / Issue weekly progress reports * Attend weekly construction / design / Permit meetings * Report to Project Manager on project progress * General Admin, document control and site procedures **Requirements** * Relevant third level qualification essential; * Valid Safepass / Manual handling certificates required for successful applicants; * 0\-1 years’ experience post qualification desirable; * Knowledge of a contracting environment would be advantageous; * Ability to work both individually and as part of a multi\-functional team essential; * Excellent IT Skills essential; * Excellent communication and organisation skills essential; **Graduate Development Programme** As part of our commitment to your growth and career development, you will be enrolled in our comprehensive 2\-year Graduate Development Programme. This programme includes experiential learning, mentoring, professional development courses, and in\-house training on key skills such as technical writing, presentation, and time management. You will benefit from peer to peer learning and receive support for obtaining professional certifications. This structured approach ensures you gain the necessary skills and experience to progress in your career. **Benefits** Be part of our Graduate Programme and contribute to meaningful projects that drive innovation and sustainability across multiple industries. We offer a competitive salary package commensurate with experience and qualifications. In addition, you will have the opportunity to work in a dynamic and supportive environment, collaborating with industry leaders and contributing to a sustainable future. Our company values work\-life balance and encourages professional growth through training and development programmes. If you are ready to make a difference and be part of a growing industry, please submit your CV, along with a cover letter highlighting your relevant experience and why you are interested in this role. Recognising the contributions and respecting our people is core to our culture and values. We are an equal opportunities employer, and we encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply for roles. Please note, we are currently not accepting applications from agencies, for this role at this time

title: Graduate Site Engineers / Geospatial Surveyors 2026

title: Graduate Trainee Accountant
description: **Vacancy Name** Graduate Trainee Accountant **Vacancy No** VN1725 **Employment Type** Permanent **Duration** N/A **Location City** Santry **Location Country** Ireland **Company Description** Xeinadin was established in 2019 when a number of leading business advisory and accountancy practices across the UK and Ireland came together to re\-imagine the future of accountancy. Our collective mission to provide locally forged, trusted business advice to SMEs through forward\-thinking, close\-knit relationships remains pivotal to our growth. It's our people who drive our business forward, and we offer them future\-focused career opportunities whilst supporting individual specialisms. Our regional offices of over 3000 colleagues operate collaboratively, combining collective expertise to maximise potential. **Description** Our Northwood office is an expanding hub, and our graduates enjoy working across a range of accounting services there, including tax and management accounts. Their role involves assisting senior colleagues in delivering high\-quality accounting services to clients, whilst developing their core accounting skills. They get invaluable training and mentoring, and can develop an exciting and rewarding career pathway, whilst studying towards professional qualification. **Key Responsibilities** Assist in the preparation of limited company and sole trader accounts, ensuring accuracy and compliance with accounting standards. Manage business and self\-assessment tax filings for clients, ensuring timely completion. Contribute to the preparation of corporation tax returns, VAT returns, and management accounts. Liaise with clients to collect relevant information for accounting and audit work. Contribute to a team environment that fosters collaboration and supports each other in achieving client and business goals. **Key Requirements** A BA in accounting or business related degree, strong academic results and presentation skills. **Additional Requirements** A desire to train in accounting and develop a career in an expanding professional services firm. **Model** Office Based **Salary** Competitive **Benefits** Benefits * Company Pension Scheme * 25 days of annual leave \+ bank holidays * Additional annual leave days from certain levels of seniority * Ability to buy up to 5 days of annual leave to reach a maximum of 30 days per annum * Business closure over Christmas\* * Life Assurance x4 annual salary * Enhanced family leave policies * Enhanced Company Sick Pay * Employee Assistance Programme – 24/7 support, free and confidential * Corporate Discounts Platform Flexible Benefits platform with ability to opt\-in to various insurances (level of seniority dependent \& self\-funded at corporate rates) such as: * PMI single or family * Critical Illness Cover * Cash plan * Cycle to work * Eye care * Dental * subject to exceptions and business needs

title: HR Generalist - 1+ Years Experience
description: **HR Generalist** My client, a high growth financial services firm based in Dublin, has partnered exclusively with re:work to recruit a Junior HR generalist. This is a hands\-on, people\-facing role reporting directly to the Head of HR and sitting at the centre of a people\-first business that has doubled in size over the past year. **What Is On Offer** * Salary: €40,000 \- €45,000 depending on experience * 10% performance bonus * Hybrid working: 4 days from home, 1 day in office per week * 25 days annual leave * Employer pension contribution * Income protection and death in service **Role Summary** This is a broad generalist role covering the full employee lifecycle, with a particular focus on recruitment, onboarding, HR administration, and culture and engagement. The successful candidate will support the Head of HR in keeping day\-to\-day operations running smoothly, while contributing to the continued development of an award\-winning employee experience. It suits someone early in their HR career who is energetic, organised, and genuinely passionate about people. **About the Company and HR Team** Our client is a well\-established and rapidly scaling financial business, with significant further growth planned. Their people function has been built from the ground up by a highly regarded Head of HR. The culture is high\-trust, flexible, and employee\-centric. This is the first dedicated HR hire into the business, so there is real scope to grow alongside the company as it scales. **What You Will Be Doing** * Supporting end\-to\-end recruitment activity including scheduling, candidate communications, and coordinating a smooth onboarding experience for every new hire * Maintaining employee records and managing the HRIS system (HR Locker) with accuracy and care * Acting as a first point of contact for employee queries, providing clear, people\-focused support * Contributing to culture and engagement initiatives that underpin the company's standout workplace reputation * Coordinating company\-wide events and off\-sites, including two annual events held outside the office * Supporting day\-to\-day office management including suppliers and the workplace environment **What We Are Looking For** * At least 1 year of experience in an HR or people support role * High energy, proactive, and genuinely passionate about people and culture * Comfortable working at pace in a small, fast\-moving team where no two days look the same * Strong organisational skills and sharp attention to detail * A natural communicator who builds relationships easily at all levels * Working towards or holding an HR qualification; CIPD membership is a plus **How They Work** This is a hybrid role with one fixed day in the Dublin city centre office each week. The company operates on a high\-trust, flexible model and genuinely lives its work\-life balance values from the top down. There is zero expectation of late nights or weekend availability. **How to Apply** Please ensure your CV is updated before applying. Due to the high volume of applications, we are unable to accept submissions via email or LinkedIn messages. This role is being managed by Alexandra Rickard, a specialist HR Recruiter across Dublin and the broader Irish market. Whether it's a single key hire or building an entire People function, if your company is hiring, feel free to connect on LinkedIn **: Alexandra Rickard \| HR Recruiter.**

title: Human Resources Administrator
description: **HR Administrator/Graduate** Dublin \| Full\-time, Permanent **About the Role** The HR Administrator will provide efficient and proactive administrative support across HR operations and recruitment activities. This role is central to ensuring smooth day\-to\-day HR processes and delivering a positive employee experience from onboarding through to exit. The successful candidate will be highly organised, detail\-oriented, and comfortable managing multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment **Key Responsibilities** **HR Administration** * Maintain accurate and up\-to\-date employee records in line with GDPR and company policy. * Prepare employment contracts, offer letters, amendments, and HR correspondence. * Support onboarding and offboarding processes, including inductions and exit documentation. * Manage probation review schedules and support managers with required documentation. * Track annual leave, sickness absence, and other employee records. * Assist with payroll administration, ensuring timely and accurate submission of information. * Support HR reporting and preparation of monthly HR metrics/KPIs. * Coordinate training records and support learning \& development administration. * Ensure HR policies and procedures are correctly documented and communicated. **Recruitment Support** * Post job advertisements across relevant platforms. * Screen CVs and coordinate interviews with hiring managers. * Manage candidate communications and ensure a positive candidate experience. * Conduct reference checks and right\-to\-work documentation checks. * Support the preparation of recruitment reports and hiring trackers. * Assist with offer preparation and onboarding documentation. **Skills \& Experience** * Bachelor’s degree in Human Resource Management or related discipline. * Strong organisational skills with high attention to detail. * Ability to handle confidential information with discretion. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills. * Proficient in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook). * Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines. * Comfortable working independently and as part of a team. **Why Join Us** * Opportunity to develop within a growing HR function * Exposure to recruitment, HR operations, and office management * Supportive and collaborative team environment * Strong opportunity for career progression in HR

title: Implementation Consultant (SaaS, €75k)
description: If you thrive on delivering complex, technical solutions, enjoy building things from scratch, and are ready to take ownership of post\-sale success for enterprise clients \- this is your next challenge. You're applying for a fast\-growing, globally trusted software (Series D, $100m ARR) company helping large organisations implement critical, highly customised solutions who are looking for a **Senior Implementation Consultant** to lead end\-to\-end delivery for strategic enterprise clients You'll be working directly with senior stakeholders and cross\-functional teams. It's a **hands\-on, high\-impact post\-sale delivery** , where your technical expertise, problem\-solving, and ability to thrive in ambiguity will drive success. **What You’ll Own:** * Lead **end\-to\-end post\-sale implementations** for enterprise clients. * Manage timelines, risks, and escalations while delivering **highly customised solutions** . * Collaborate closely with Engineering, Product, Account Management, and Customer Success teams. * Translate complex customer requirements into **practical, technical implementations** . * Resolve complex technical and delivery issues, ensuring smooth, timely outcomes. * Contribute to and improve implementation methodologies and best practices. * Mentor and support junior implementation team members. **What You Bring:** * 5\+ years implementing software for **enterprise or strategic customers** . * Proven track record delivering **complex, technical, post\-sale implementations** . * Direct customer\-facing experience with **large global organisations** (e.g., Booking.com, WorldPay). * Strong experience with **APIs (RESTful)** , JSON, JavaScript, SQL, and relational databases. * Comfortable managing multiple implementations in **ambiguous, first\-time delivery scenarios** . * Strong problem\-solving skills and **technical acumen** . * Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Software, or related field preferred; formal PM certification is a plus but not essential. * Experience in SME or scale\-up environments preferred \- **mindset matters more than brand** . **The good stuff:** * **Location:** Dublin (Hybrid – Tues/Wed/Thurs in office) * **Type:** Full\-time, permanent * **Salary:** Up to €75k \& benefits If you've read this far, it means there is clearly a level of interest and you should apply (If you are the right person of course!) If you want to **own enterprise post\-sale delivery** , work with complex systems, and make an impact from day one: APPLY NOW!

title: Investment Finance Associate (Private Credit)
description: **We are supporting a leading global investment firm to appoint a Finance Associate / Senior Finance Associate.** **This role is ideal for Big‑4 professionals seeking their first move into industry, or someone with 1\-2 years' PQE offering exposure to complex investment structures, financial reporting, and cross‑functional collaboration within a sophisticated alternatives platform.** **Responsibilities:** * Support senior finance leadership across a range of fund‑level reporting and operational finance activities. * Review investment structures and assess accounting, tax, and regulatory considerations. * Assist with investor reporting, fund performance analysis, and ad‑hoc information requests. * Contribute to the preparation and review of quarterly and annual financial statements. * Coordinate audit processes and liaise with external advisors. * Support reviews of valuations, capital activity, and fee calculations. **Requirements:** * Newly Qualified Accountant from Big‑4 audit. (FS audit, asset management, banking, or structured finance advantageous). Or 1\-2 years' PQE in funds. * Strong technical grounding in accounting, financial reporting, and controls. * Excellent analytical skills and strong attention to detail. * Comfortable interpreting complex financial documentation. * Confident communicator with the ability to work collaboratively across teams. * Advanced Excel skills; professional qualification (ACA/ACCA/CPA) required or nearing completion. **This is a great opportunity to step directly into the finance team of a global investment organisation, gaining exposure to alternative investment strategies, sophisticated fund structures, and a high‑performance international environment. The role is Dublin‑based with hybrid working and strong long‑term career progression.** Apply in confidence or contact Matthew Fleming at mfleming@elevatepartners.ie.

title: Junior Health and Safety Advisor
description: **Ayrton Group** are seeking an **EHS Advisor** to join us on a project with a leading pharmaceutical client for a project based in the Dublin area (Dún Laoghaire / Poolbeg region). This is an exciting opportunity to join a high\-performing project team within a highly regulated and safety\-critical environment. **Key Responsibilities:** * Assist the EHS Manager in ensuring full compliance with all Health, Safety \& Workplace Legislation. * Work effectively with the Project Manager and Construction Team in the implementation of the Integrated Management System. * Ensure the site is adequately set up with a Site\-Specific Construction EHS Plan. * Become a Subject Matter Expert (SME) in high\-risk activities and ensure they are identified and effectively controlled on site. * Assist with the development of Risk Assessments and Method Statements. * Support the development of SOPs and deliver training and communication to site personnel. * Promote best practices and a strong health \& safety culture across the project. * Report and investigate incidents, ensuring root causes are identified, mitigated, and communicated. **Key Requirements:** * Suitable Candidates must have Relevant Health and Safety Diploma / Degree and Minimum of 1 year experience in Environmental Health and Safety role. * Excellent oral and written communication skills. * Ability to prioritize workload according to deadlines together with a high attention to detail. * Ability to influence colleagues at all levels. * Enjoy working as part of a high performing team. * Experience within EP\&R environments is desired but not a must.

title: Junior Pastry Chef
description: Kennedy's Food Store is one of the city's leading independent Café \& catering businesses. We’re a growing business with a close\-knit team of creative chefs. We are ambitious about our food and make everything in our own kitchens from scratch. We are hiring a **Junior Pastry Chef** to work alongside, supporting and following direction from our Senior Pastry Chef in the National Botanic Gardens. The role will be based on an initial 6 month contract. The person who will fill this role will; * Have experience in a pastry led environment * Understand and champion food presentation \& finishing of pastry * Be dependable \& hardworking * Approach work with positivity and work in a small team * Work a 30/35 hour week, daytime only hours Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €14\.15\-€15\.00 per hour Expected hours: 35 per week Benefits: * Employee discount * Flexitime Work Location: In person

title: Junior Sales Executive
description: **Position Description:** The Junior Sales Executive supports Cloud Picker's commercial growth by identifying new business opportunities, nurturing prospect relationships, and assisting in the delivery of sales activities across wholesale and retail channels. Working closely with and shadowing the Sales Manager, this role is designed to develop a well\-rounded commercial skillset within the specialty coffee industry. **Job Specifications:** **Location:** Dublin, Ireland **Reports to:** Sales Manager **Responsibilities:** * Drive revenue growth by targeting high\-value accounts and strategic partnerships. * Maintain and upkeep CRM and all sales databases, while providing sufficient reporting to Senior Management * Identify and pursue new sales opportunities in the specialty coffee market under the direction of the Sales Manager. * Nurture and develop relationships with prospects throughout the sales funnel. * Support upselling opportunities to existing clients in collaboration with the Sales Manager and Wholesale Account Executives. * Lead presentation on product offerings and preparing customised proposals to meet client needs, including organising and conducting coffee tastings. * Work toward monthly, quarterly, and annual sales targets agreed with the Sales Manager. * Support wholesale account executives by ensuring they have accurate client onboarding materials and are maintaining contact within our one\-month onboarding window. * Assist the Sales Manager in preparing weekly and monthly sales reports for management. * Contribute to a cross\-functional team environment. * Identify new wholesale and retail products for Cloud Picker to present, ensuring Cloud Picker is at the forefront of coffee innovation. * Manage and curate the retail equipment range, ensuring inventory remains current, relevant, and well stocked. **Essential Skills and Knowledge:** * 1\-3 years of experience in a sales, hospitality, or customer\-facing role. * Strong interpersonal and communication skills \- confident on the phone and in person. * Highly organised, with strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple tasks. * Comfortable working both independently and as part of a small, fast\-moving team. * Full, clean driver's licence. * Degree or equivalent qualification in Business, Marketing, or a related field. * An interest in or knowledge of specialty coffee, FMCG, or food \& beverage. * Familiarity with CRM tools or sales reporting. **B Corporation** Cloud Picker is committed to B Corporation and the certificate and values it holds. Thus, our employees are also committed to acting on behalf of our agreement with the B Corporation values. Our actions are: **General:** * Motivated to make processes more efficient, safe, sustainable and transparent. * Oriented towards cooperation by involving peers in their work when relevant, asking for feedback when necessary and effectively processing said feedback in a productive manner. * Committed to (advancing the awareness of) health and safety policies, such as respecting the workload and office hours of peers. * Attentive to new ways our business operations can be leveraged to further positive environmental or social impact. * Proactive in sharing ideas on how to improve (business) practices, engage external stakeholders or support the communities in which we operate. **Social:** * Engagement of stakeholders beyond (direct) sales partners, eg. community stakeholders where relevant. * Engagement of stakeholders on topics beyond sales\-related conversations, eg. to proactively open up conversations with stakeholders on how to minimise waste, conserve energy or water, and make more sustainable purchasing or operating decisions. * Supportive of our local communities wherever possible through local sourcing, hiring and investment. **Environmental:** * Willing to put in extra effort to gather, process and disclose accurate information regarding environmental and/or social impact to internal and external stakeholders. * Committed to minimising waste and correctly processing the waste that is generated. **Cloud Picker is an Equal an Opportunity Employer:** At Cloud Picker Coffee, quality is the core of our operations. We know that the quality of our coffee depends on the well\-being of the people and places we work with. This is why our business is founded on a set of values that aims to create shared value and well\-being for all. We do business with integrity and respect: We conduct our business based on honesty and ethical decision\-making. We respect human rights, the interests of our stakeholders and individual differences. We are committed to operating sustainably and continuously challenging ourselves to do better. We govern our business in full compliance with all applicable laws, rules and regulations. We encourage curiosity and celebrate diversity. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €37,500\.00\-€40,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Employee assistance program * Employee discount * Private dental insurance * Private medical insurance Work Location: In person

title: Junior Site Engineer
description: **Junior Site Engineer – Civil Infrastructure** A leading civil engineering contractor is seeking a **Graduate / Junior Site Engineer** to join their growing team across projects in **Meath** and surrounding areas. This is an excellent opportunity for a final\-year student or recent graduate to gain hands\-on experience on large\-scale civil infrastructure and public realm projects. **Key Responsibilities** * Assist with the delivery of civil infrastructure projects including roads, cycle lanes, drainage, and public realm upgrades * Support site setting out, surveying, and quality control * Monitor project progress and report to senior engineers and project managers * Coordinate with subcontractors and site teams * Ensure compliance with health \& safety standards and company procedures * Assist in preparing site reports and documentation **Requirements** * Degree in Civil Engineering (or related discipline) – graduating May/June or recently qualified * **Must have completed some form of work placement or internship during studies (even short\-term), ideally within civil engineering, construction, or utilities** * Strong communication and organisational skills * Willingness to work on\-site across multiple locations * Basic knowledge of AutoCAD or Civil 3D is an advantage * Full driving licence preferred **What’s on Offer** * **€40k–€45k starting salary** * Exposure to major civil and public realm projects * Structured mentoring and career development * Clear progression pathway within a leading contractor **To apply:** If you are a motivated Graduate / Junior Engineer looking to kick\-start your career, please send your CV or contact Amy Maguire for a confidential discussion. Email: a.maguire@gpcireland.com *GPC will not send your CV to clients without prior discussion and consent.*

title: Learning & Development Executive
description: A leading international law firm is seeking a talented Learning \& Development Executive to join its high\-performing HR \& People Development team. This is a fantastic opportunity to play a key role in shaping the growth and development of legal talent within a dynamic and collaborative environment. You will support the delivery of innovative training programmes, helping trainees and junior lawyers reach their full potential. Working closely with senior stakeholders, you will contribute to the design and execution of impactful learning initiatives aligned with business goals. The role offers exposure to a wide range of development activities, from structured programmes to performance management support. Based in Dublin, this position is available on a full\-time, 4\-day in office basis. **Key Responsibilities** * Coordinate and deliver the firm’s in\-house trainee training programme in line with professional requirements * Manage and enhance established learning programmes for trainees and junior associates * Support performance review processes and development planning initiatives * Design, deliver and evaluate training solutions aligned to individual and business needs * Manage external training providers, ensuring quality and cost\-effectiveness **Key Requirements** * 3–5 years’ experience in a Learning \& Development role * Strong experience designing and delivering training programmes and workshops * Ability to build relationships and influence stakeholders at all levels * Excellent communication, facilitation and mentoring skills * Degree qualified in HR, Law, Business, Psychology or related field

title: Marketing Associate
description: **The Role** PE Global are recruiting for a **Marketing Associate** for our biopharmaceutical client based in Santry, Dublin. This is an initial 12\-month contract role. Remote role. **Job Responsibilities** * Contribute to product branding, communications, and promotional activities to support brand strategy and execution * Coordinate effectively with brand, sales, and cross\-functional teams to ensure aligned and high\-quality delivery * Define and deliver marketing projects, with guidance from the line manager * Provide marketing support across a portfolio of products, ensuring consistent execution of key initiatives * Manage and track cross\-functional budgets against plan, proactively identifying and addressing any deviations * Plan and organise cross\-functional Brand Team meetings to enable effective decision\-making and alignment * Support the planning and execution of congresses, ensuring strong presence and seamless delivery * Act as Responsible Employee for domestic and international sponsored meetings, ensuring compliant and efficient end\-to\-end logistics * Coordinate and deliver multichannel activities, including digital and print campaigns, to maximise customer engagement * Lead materials management, ensuring accuracy, availability, and compliance of all promotional materials * Develop high\-quality promotional materials in partnership with the Brand Lead and line manager, working closely with external agencies and the service centre * Drive materials through the PromoMats approval process in a timely and compliant title: Mechanical Estimator

title: Office & People Operations Executive
description: **Job Title: Office \& People Operations Executive** **Location:** Dublin, Ranelagh (hybrid) **A bit about Sons:** **Sons** was founded in 2019 by Will and Adrian, driven by their own struggles with hair loss and the lack of accessible, affordable treatment options. Today, Sons is a certified **B Corp digital health company** on a mission to make men’s health more open, optimistic, and effective. We bring together physicians, pharmacists, scientists, technologists, and creatives to tackle the reality that most men don’t seek help for issues like hair loss. Through our telehealth platform and cloud pharmacy, we deliver clinically proven treatments straight to their doors—safely, affordably, and discreetly. With over **300,000 men treated** across the UK, Ireland, and Germany, and a Series A **€6\.75M funding round** , we’re expanding into new markets and channels. Our goal? To help every generation of sons live healthier, happier lives. **A bit out the job:** We’re looking for an Office \& People Operations Executive to be the heartbeat of our Dublin office, someone who genuinely cares about people, thrives on creating a great workplace experience, and believes that culture doesn’t just happen, it’s built. This is an entry level position at the intersection of people, culture and office operations, supporting day\-to\-day People Ops while taking real ownership of how our culture shows up — in the office, in onboarding, in team moments, and in the small details that make a big difference. You’ll be a go\-to person for employees, a culture champion, and a calm organiser behind the scenes. **What You’ll Be Doing** **People \& Operations** * Support core People Ops processes including onboarding, offboarding, contracts, documentation and employee records * Be a first point of contact for people\-related queries, escalating where needed * Support HR processes such as probation tracking, absence management and policy administration * Help keep People data and systems accurate and up to date **Office \& Workplace Experience** * Keep the Dublin office running smoothly and feeling welcoming, organised and well\-stocked * Coordinate office logistics, suppliers and general facilities needs * Ensure the office is a place people *want* to come into, not have to **Culture, Engagement \& Employee Experience** * Help bring our company values to life through day‑to‑day actions and initiatives * Organise team events, celebrations, social moments and culture initiatives * Support engagement surveys, wellbeing initiatives and internal communications * Be a friendly, approachable presence in the office — someone people naturally come to * Champion inclusion, connection and a positive employee experience **A bit about you** * A genuine interest in **people, culture and employee experience** * Highly organised with great attention to detail * Confident communicator who builds trust easily * Proactive, positive and happy to roll up their sleeves * Someone who notices the small things — and cares enough to fix them **Why Join Us?** * A people\-first company where culture genuinely matters * A role with real ownership and visibility * Supportive leadership and a collaborative team * Space to grow and develop within People Operations * A workplace where your impact will be felt every day **Diversity, Equity, \& Inclusion** At Sons, we are committed to fostering a diverse, equitable, and inclusive workplace where every individual is valued and respected. Our recruitment and selection process is guided by policies that ensure non\-discrimination, merit\-based promotion, alignment of job descriptions with equal opportunities principles, fair treatment for all applicants, regular review of selection criteria, panel interviewing for objectivity, non\-discriminatory interview questions, assistance with application forms as needed, and unbiased selection decisions. We believe that diversity drives innovation and creativity, and we are dedicated to creating an environment where every employee has equal opportunities for growth and success **What we offer:** * 26 days holiday (plus bank holidays throughout the year) * Work form anywhere policy * Flexy working hours policy * Private Medical Insurance via VHI * Free access to AvivaCare * Regular team socials and yearly offsite * Discounts to Sons products for you, your family and friends

title: Operational Analyst - Capacity Funding
description: **Operational Analyst – Capacity Funding \& Delivery** We are the people behind Ireland’s electricity grid. We operate the power system with world leading levels of variable asynchronous renewable generation, ensuring that everyone has power when they need it. But that’s not all, we are now transforming the power system for future generations. And that’s just the beginning of our story. We have a critical purpose. The purpose is to transform the power system to enable our society to reduce our carbon emissions and meet our climate change and net zero ambitions. Be part of the transformation! **The Opportunity** The Operations \& Asset Management Office is at the heart of Ireland’s electricity system, bringing together expert professionals from engineering and commercial backgrounds. We’re central to delivering a secure, reliable, and sustainable power system and wholesale electricity market for the island of Ireland — and above all, ensuring the lights stay on. Our mission is clear: support Ireland’s transition to 80% renewable electricity by 2030 and net zero by 2050\. We manage real\-time system and market operations, ensure financial settlement excellence, and engage directly with market participants. Through continuous improvement and innovation, we’re shaping a resilient, future\-ready energy system that meets the evolving needs of all electricity users. We’re now seeking an Operational Analyst to join our team — a fantastic opportunity to become part of the Capacity Market Auctions team. As Operational Analyst, you will work collaboratively across the Group, engaging effectively with key stakeholders, to bring about the change necessary to deliver on our strategic ambitions. You will use your technical, report writing and verbal communications skills to continuously improve the way we work, making it more efficient and effective as well as transparent and easy to understand for both technical and non\-technical audiences. We will look to you to support the development of tools to assist with day to day operation of Capacity Market as well as assess the technical qualification and auction outcomes. In your role, you will analyse and present external and internal data / analysis to identify important trends so that it can be easily understood and utilised by the organisation, DECC, Regulators and auction participants. You will participate in User Acceptance Testing for new system releases as well as develop business processes to support day to day Capacity Market. **The Team** The Capacity Funding \& Delivery team runs the auctions that secure the long\-term capacity needed to keep the lights on and support Ireland’s transition to a low\-carbon future, in line with the Climate Action Plan. Administering auctions with the utmost integrity is a core mandate of this team. We are committed to securing capacity to ensure the lights stay on and to delivering the climate commitments set out by the Government. Our environment is fun, fast\-paced, and collaborative, where we respect each other for the contributions we make in achieving our purpose. **About You** Successful candidates will have: * Third level qualification in a relevant discipline * Typically 2\-4 years relevant technical experience * Excellent presentation skills * Effective communicator and excellent report writing capability * Experience of establishing and maintaining effective relationships with internal and external stakeholders * Ability to work effectively within a team environment with a flexible approach * Strong attention to detail and quality orientation * Strong numerical, analytical and problem solving capability **If you believe you have the skills and experience relevant for this position, please upload your CV and a cover letter (in one document). We want to hear from you.** **Closing date for applications** 10th April 2026 **We're dedicated to diversity and inclusion at Eirgrid. We recognise the strength that comes from having a diverse workforce and the importance of a supportive culture for all our people to achieve their potential. Our diversity and inclusion networks have been designed to create an inclusive culture that enables our people to feel a sense of belonging at work.** **As an equal opportunities** **’** **employer, we welcome applications from people of all backgrounds. Reasonable accommodations are offered at every stage of our recruitment process**

title: Operations Associate, GTM Accelerate
description: **About Stripe** Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. **About The Team** The Professional Services team exists to drive the adoption of Stripe’s capabilities across our user base, from Fortune 100s to high\-growth startups. Within this group, the Accelerate team is responsible for the critical "zero\-to\-one" phase of the user journey. We partner with developers and technical leaders to ensure they deploy quickly, integrate with high quality, and derive strategic value from Stripe immediately. Accelerate is Stripe’s dedicated onboarding program designed to get users live fast and operationally ready. It is a prescriptive engagement where we guide users through technical integration, fraud prevention setup, and financial reporting. Whether through our foundational (free) tier or our premium high\-touch service, the goal is identical: eliminate friction, reduce implementation risk, and accelerate time\-to\-revenue. **What You’ll Do** Stripe is launching Stripe Delivery Centers \- a brand new global team to design, implement and grow Stripe’s operations for the next decade. We are looking for dynamic and curious people that have a passion for solving global user issues, building operations, driving process improvements and want to play a front\-line role in building this new operational capability for Stripe and accelerating Stripe’s growth. If you like challenging, scaled problems and are an amazing teammate, we want to hear from you! As an Onboarding Specialist, you are the technical guide for users during their first 90 days with Stripe. You will be proactively unblocking deployments and ensuring users are operationally ready to scale. You will manage a portfolio of users, guiding them through best practices for integration, dashboard configuration, and risk management. You will deliver technical workshops and serve as the primary touchpoint to help users navigate the complexity of payments infrastructure. **Responsibilities** * Drive Time\-to\-Value: Manage a portfolio of user implementations, acting as a lightweight project manager to ensure they hit their planned go\-live dates. * Deliver Technical Workshops: Lead pre\-built, interactive workshops covering critical topics such as API integration, Dashboard utilization, Fraud/Radar configuration, and Financial Reporting. * Proactive Unblocking: Anticipate technical challenges before they become blockers. Provide prescriptive guidance on API architecture and best practices to ensure a high\-quality integration. * Operational Readiness: Go beyond code—help users set up their teams for success by advising on dispute management workflows and reconciliation processes. * User Experience: Provide exceptional customer service to ensure a positive onboarding experience, acting as a reliable partner so users feel supported and confident throughout the process. * Continuous Learning: Continuously update your knowledge of Stripe's products, features, and updates to ensure you are providing the most current and strategic advice to your users. * Process Improvement: Contribute to the improvement of onboarding processes and documentation by collaborating with cross\-functional teams to refine our playbooks based on user feedback. **Who You Are** We are looking for dynamic, curious problem\-solvers who love demystifying complex technical concepts. You thrive in a fast\-paced environment and are passionate about helping users build their businesses on Stripe. **Minimum Requirements** * Advanced English proficiency (fluent written and spoken). You can explain complex API architecture to non\-technical stakeholders and discuss business value with developers. * 2\+ years of experience in implementation consulting, technical support, or solutions engineering, preferably in API technologies or SaaS environments. * Technical Aptitude: Strong ability to read and debug code, understand RESTful APIs, and navigate complex technical environments. * Problem Solving: You don't just find the answer; you find the best solution for the user's specific business model. **Preferred Qualifications** * Proficiency in one or more of: French, German, Spanish or Italian. * Experience with financial systems (ERP, Accounting), online payments, or e\-commerce platforms. * Experience delivering live technical training or webinars to external users. * Familiarity with code\-level debugging in languages such as Python, Ruby, or Node.js. This role is based in Dublin, Ireland and requires in\-office attendance. In\-office expectations Office\-assigned Stripes in most of our locations are currently expected to spend at least 50% of the time in a given month in their local office or with users. This expectation may vary depending on role, team and location. For example, Stripes in Stripe Delivery Center roles in Mexico City, Mexico and Bengaluru, India work 100% from the office. Also, some teams have greater in\-office attendance requirements, to appropriately support our users and workflows, which the hiring manager will discuss. This approach helps strike a balance between bringing people together for in\-person collaboration and learning from each other, while supporting flexibility when possible. Pay and benefits The annual salary range for this role in the primary location is €36,000 \- €66,000\. This range may change if you are hired in another location. For sales roles, the range provided is the role’s On Target Earnings (“OTE”) range, meaning that the range includes both the sales commissions/sales bonuses target and annual base salary for the role. This salary range may be inclusive of several career levels at Stripe and will be narrowed during the interview process based on a number of factors, including the candidate’s experience, qualifications, and specific location. Applicants interested in this role and who are not located in the primary location may request the annual salary range for their location during the interview process. Specific benefits and details about what compensation is included in the salary range listed above will vary depending on the applicant’s location and can be discussed in more detail during the interview process. Benefits/additional compensation for this role may include: equity, company bonus or sales commissions/bonuses; retirement plans; health benefits; and wellness stipends.

title: Operations Summer Intern
description: ***About the Role*** As a Student Operations Intern, you will support the full lifecycle of a Greystar student community. This role blends on\-site resident hospitality with operational and commercial management, focusing on the unique needs of the student demographic and the critical Summer period. Please note that this is a 10\-week internship during June and August. ***Key Responsibilities*** * **Full Lifecycle Support:** Own the administrative journey from initial enquiry and room viewings to contract signing, guarantor verification, and the high\-intensity "Summer Turn" periods. * **Front\-of\-House Query Management:** Act as the first point of contact for all resident enquiries, resolving issues ranging from parcel collection and maintenance requests to account queries with speed and empathy. * **Leasing \& Compliance Admin:** Maintain accurate resident records within property management systems ensuring all tenancy documentation and right\-to\-rent checks meet compliance standards. * **Summer Short\-Stay Operations:** Support the transition to short\-term tenancies during the summer months, managing high\-frequency check\-ins for interns and summer school groups to maximize asset occupancy. * **Head Office Operational Support:** Bridge the gap between site and Head Office by assisting the Operations team with portfolio\-wide data entry, occupancy reporting, and process improvement projects. * **Operational \& Financial Admin:** Support lease processing, guarantor verification, and rent collection. Gain exposure to reporting, financial and compliance standards (Maintenance/Facilities/Health \& Safety) to ensure asset protection. ***About You*** * **The Student Expert:** Currently enrolled in a degree (Real Estate, Hospitality, or Business) with an interest in the Living sector. * **Agile Communicator:** Comfortable pivoting from supporting a student to presenting data to an Operations Manager * **Tech\-Fluent:** Proficient in Microsoft Office (Excel is essential) and ready to learn specialised platforms like Yardi or Entrata ***About Greystar*** *Greystar is a leading, fully integrated real estate company offering expertise in investment management, development, and management of rental housing properties globally. The company's business model is unique in its ability to own, operate, and develop multifamily, student, and senior housing across the globe. Building a global platform with a local presence, Greystar continues to expand its geographic reach around the world, bringing a globally interconnected professional rental housing platform and industry funded with institutional capital to countries where the sector does not yet exist. Greystar’s pan – European platform was established in 2013 and has scaled rapidly, now with investment, operational and development presence in the UK, Ireland, France, the Netherlands, Germany, Austria, and Spain.* *One of Greystar’s core values is Equality, and as such, we continue to pride ourselves on being an equal opportunities employer, accounting for all protected and identifiable characteristics. We truly value diversity of thought, background and of experience. Please reach out to the Talent Acquisition team to explore opportunities at Greystar in more detail or visit our careers site at https://jobs.greystar.com/.*

title: P2P Analyst

title: PSDP

title: Planner
description: Planner, Mercury Headquarters, Ballycoolin, Dublin Apply Now **Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions.** We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities, and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres, Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Principal Duties And Responsibilities** * Responsible for a portfolio of tender projects * Reporting to Planning Department * Tender Schedule Development * Prepare comprehensive tender schedules (Level 2 to 4\), schedule narratives, and progress curves aligned with bid requirements. * Ensure programmes align with Schedule Specifications, Employer Requirements, contract conditions and Internal SOP/Guidelines. * Assist with the creation of 4D models and / or 2D phasing plans to present construction sequence, logistics and phasing strategies. * Collaborate with Internal teams to clarify milestones, scope, sequencing and construction methodology. * Planning \& Scheduling * Develop tender programmes across all phases including design, procurement, preconstruction, construction, commissioning and handover phases. Perform delay analysis, float path reviews, and milestone projections. * Data Management \& Reporting * Maintain planning databases and produce progress reports for internal and client review. * Provide accurate data for payment applications and contract reviews. * Stakeholder Engagement * Act as the primary point of contact for tender planning queries. Liaise with clients, subcontractors, and internal teams to ensure alignment by assisting in schedule workshop meetings. * Attend mid\-bid meetings to present Tender Schedule and deliverables as required. * Resource \& Cost Loading * Apply resource and cash flow loading to schedules in coordination with estimators. * Leadership \& Training * Mentor junior planners and promote best practices in planning processes. Provide cover for Planners responsibilities when required. * Compliance \& Governance * Ensure adherence to company SOPs and client standards for all planning deliverables. * Making relevant parties aware of issues and advising on solutions * Training staff if required * Covering Senior Tender Planner responsibilities when required * All other duties associated with role **Essential Criteria For The Role** * Ability to carry out the roles and responsibilities as defined above * Background in construction\-related degree or trade * 5\+ years’ experience in Project Planning of large construction projects * Excellent communication skills * Excellent IT and software skills (including Oracle P6 and Excel) * Proven ability to work effectively in a team * Proven ability to work within strict deadlines * Knowledge of and commitment to Health \& Safety ***Mercury is an equal opportunities employer.*** Apply Now

title: R&D Tax Intern
description: **Application Deadline:** 30 April 2026 **Department:** Tax (Direct) **Location:** Dublin **Description** At BDO Ireland, we encourage you to be yourself at work every day, empowering you to learn and make a real impact on our clients' businesses. As an R\&D Tax Intern, you’ll collaborate with diverse sectors, including software, electronics, and biotechnology, to optimize their R\&D activities, secure funding, and prepare financial calculations for R\&D tax credits. This role offers an excellent opportunity for a Computer Science, Engineering, Physics Student or a student in a related field to learn in a dynamic and supportive environment, where you’ll be involved in everything from client interviews to audit support, helping drive innovation and sustainable technologies. You’ll gain valuable experience preparing documentation for Irish Revenue and liaising with grant\-awarding bodies, while also identifying additional R\&D funding opportunities for our clients. title: Regional Business Support Administrator - Ireland
description: **About Us** Our purpose is simple: to save lives at sea. 24 hours a day, every day, RNLI lifesavers are ready to launch to the rescue. We’re looking for a Regional Business Support Administrator to join our Business Support Team in Ireland. The Business Support Team supports the RNLI through front\-line support services and guidance to volunteers and staff in the Ireland Region. Some of the benefits * Salary \- €31,659 to €37,246 / £25,877 to £30,444 (dependent on experience) * Flexible working * 26 days’ annual leave plus Bank Holidays * Outstanding pension scheme * Life assurance * Health plan Your role **As a Regional Business Support Administrator You Will** * Act as a regional point of contact for volunteers and staff for planned and reactive support and guidance ensuring continuity of service. * Resolve requests from Regional Teams using initiative to resolve issues and support requirements, ensuring an appropriate level of customer service. * Accurately and efficiently process tasks and maintain records utilising various in\-house systems. * Liaise with suppliers and stakeholders to create stock, non\-stock orders and process payments in line with Standing Financial Instructions whilst demonstrating commercial awareness. * Organise travel, accommodation and expenses requirements for volunteers adopting a proactive approach to minimise costs whilst adhering to budgetary guidelines. * Processing of relief payments in line with defined procedures and coordinate relief cover as required to maintain service. * Provide support in the planning and coordination of meetings and events including note taking. **About You** You’ll be a positive ‘people person’ able to work to reactive and planned workloads and pressures in a fast\-paced environment. You will have strong customer service and communication skills, able to build rapport with volunteers and staff. To be considered for the Regional Business Support Administrator you will need: * To be qualified to GCSE/Junior Certificate level or relevant experience. * IT skills including Microsoft Office, customer service training or experience. * Experience of working with volunteers would be of benefit. If you have the skills and experience to be our Regional Business Support Administrator this could be the role for you. Please apply via the button shown. Safeguarding The RNLI is committed to safeguarding; protecting a person’s health, wellbeing, and human rights, enabling them to live free from harm, abuse, and neglect. We expect all employees and volunteers to share this commitment and have a zero\-tolerance approach. The suitability of all prospective employees and volunteers will be assessed during the recruitment process in line with this commitment. This will include relevant criminal record checks being carried out dependent on the eligibility of the role. (England \& Wales; DBS check, Scotland; Disclosure Scotland PVG, Northern Ireland; Access NI, Republic of Ireland; Garda Vetting; International, International Child Protection Certificate process). Diversity at the RNLI Our staff and volunteers have been saving lives at sea without prejudice for 200 years. We respect and value diversity of background, skills and perspectives within our teams, and consider it essential to help us deliver a world\-class lifesaving service. We are an inclusive organisation and welcome applications from everyone. In addition to having the skills needed for the role, we also look for applicants who share our commitment to living our RNLI values (trustworthy, courageous, selfless, and dependable), and helping us work towards Our Vision: To save Every One.

title: Research Associate in Aeroacoustics
description: This Job is based in Australia **The Opportunity** The Flow Noise Group in the School of Mechanical and Manufacturing Engineering is seeking a Research Associate in Aeroacoustics to join a growing team advancing the understanding of flow‑induced noise. This role supports a newly awarded ARC Discovery Project investigating the noise mechanisms of ducted propellers operating in complex flow environments. You’ll contribute to high‑impact experimental aeroacoustics research, work closely with leading academics including Professor Con Doolan, and help drive new insights into turbulence, noise generation, and next‑generation propulsion technologies. This position will give you the opportunity to develop your research skills and output by contributing to the writing of scientific papers and reports for international journals, participating in conferences and workshops, assisting in the supervision of research students, and actively engaging with industry partners. The role reports to Professor Con Doolan and has no direct reports. * Salary (Level A) – AUD $113,911 to $121,838 per annum \+ 17% superannuation * Full time * Fixed\-term contract – 2\.5 years * Location: Kensington – Sydney, Australia **About Us** UNSW is a world‑leading institution recognised for its scale, prestige, and impact. With strong industry engagement and partnerships across sectors, UNSW provides a unique environment where academic expertise translates into real‑world outcomes. The university is home to cutting‑edge research that drives innovation and societal progress, while its excellence in teaching ensures students are prepared to lead in their fields. For academics, UNSW offers an outstanding platform to flourish — combining world‑class facilities, collaborative networks, and a culture of innovation that supports both career growth and meaningful contributions to the wider community. The School of Mechanical and Manufacturing Engineering that is internationally recognised for its excellence in research and teaching. Our mission is to nurture students to become industry leaders who will generate societal, economic, and environmental benefits. The School is one of the largest and most prestigious schools Australia for thriving research programs and contribution to education excellence in Aerospace, Mechanical Engineering, Advanced Manufacturing Engineering, Robotics and Mechatronic Engineering. Our Schools QS ranking for 2025 is \#46 globally and the highest in Australia. For further information on our school, go to \- https://www.unsw.edu.au/engineering/our\-schools/mechanical\-and\-manufacturing\-engineering The Flow Noise Group is a leading research team advancing the science of flow‑induced noise, aeroacoustics, and turbulent flow physics. Our work integrates advanced experimentation, modelling, and noise‑control strategies across systems such as airfoils, ducted propellers, drones, and wind‑energy devices. With strong academic and industry partnerships, our group tackles fundamental and applied challenges in how unsteady flows generate sound and how that sound can be predicted or reduced. For more information please visit \- https://www.unsw.edu.au/research/flownoise **Skills \& Experience** * A PhD in a related discipline, and/or relevant work experience * Experience in experimental aeroacoustics, turbulence measurements and/or wind tunnel testing. * Experience in high\-speed data acquisition and signal processing * Desirable but not essential to have experience in the development of analytical or theoretical aeroacoustic models * Proven commitment to proactively keeping up to date with discipline knowledge and developments. * Demonstrated ability to undertake high quality academic research and conduct independent research with limited supervision * Demonstrated track record of publications and conference presentations relative to opportunity * Demonstrated ability to work in a team, collaborate across disciplines and build effective relationships * Evidence of highly developed interpersonal skills * Demonstrated ability to communicate and interact with a diverse range of stakeholders and students * An understanding of and commitment to UNSW’s aims, objectives and values in action, together with relevant policies and guidelines * Knowledge of health and safety responsibilities and commitment to attending relevant health and safety training Additional details about the specific responsibilities for this position can be found in the position description. This is available via JOBS@UNSW. **Pre\-Employment Checks** Aligned with UNSW’s focus on cultivating a workplace defined by safety, ethical conduct, and strong integrity, preferred candidates will be required to participate in a combination of pre\-employment checks relevant to the role they have applied for. These pre\-employment checks may include a combination of some of the following checks: * National and International Criminal history checks * Entitlement to work and ID checks * Working With Children Checks * Completion of a Gender\-Based Violence Prevention Declaration * Verification of relevant qualifications * Verification of relevant professional membership * Employment history and reference checks * Financial responsibility assessments/checks. * Medical Checks and Assessments Compliance with the necessary combination of these checks is a condition of employment at UNSW. **To Apply:** Please click the apply now button and submit your CV, Cover Letter and Responses to the Skills and Experience. You should systematically address the Skills and Experience listed within the position description in your application. Please note applications will not be accepted if sent to the contact listed below. **Contact** : For role\-specific inquiries, please contact Prof Con Doolan E: c.doolan@unsw.edu.au For questions regarding the recruitment process, please contact Eugene Aves (Talent Acquisition Partner) E: eugene.aves@unsw.edu.au **Applications close:** 11:55 pm (Sydney time) on Tuesday 7 April 2026 UNSW is committed to evolving a culture that embraces equity and supports a diverse and inclusive community where everyone can participate fairly, in a safe and respectful environment. We welcome candidates from all backgrounds and encourage applications from people of diverse gender, sexual orientation, cultural and linguistic backgrounds, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander background, people with disability and those with caring and family responsibilities. UNSW provides workplace adjustments for people with disability, and access to flexible work options for eligible staff. The University reserves the right not to proceed with any appointment.

description: ***Trinity*** **Motors** Trinity Motors is seeking a motivated and detail\-oriented Trainee Bookkeeper to join our team due to continued growth.
